atk.name: Ace

gadget.name: S.E.L.M.A. Aqua Breacher
gadget.description: S.E.L.M.A. Aqua Breacher — a throwable hydraulic breaching device that uses water pressure to breach walls, floors, hatches, and other reinforced surfaces. The device uses a hydraulic spike (piton) that deploys sequentially.
mechanics: S.E.L.M.A. is thrown onto a breachable wall surface
The cylinder extends a telescopic spike (piton) and detonates once
If space allows, it lowers down and detonates a second time, creating a larger hole
Two sequential detonations create a large rectangular breach suitable for operator passage and vaulting/crouch-walking
Detonates only ONCE when thrown onto horizontal surfaces
Creates a single rectangular breach; cannot activate sequential explosions on horizontal surfaces
Reinforced hatches require TWO S.E.L.M.A. charges to fully destroy
Throws similar to Impact Grenades with extended range
tips: Use S.E.L.M.A. on breachable walls to create dual openings for flexible entry vectors
Throw S.E.L.M.A. through windows or doors to create breaches in adjacent rooms from range
Confirm wall is not electrified (Bandit/Kaid) before committing to throw
S.E.L.M.A. on floor hatches requires two charges to destroy the reinforced hatch completely
Extendable arms clear defender gadgets — use S.E.L.M.A. near clustered utility to clear area
Coordinate timing with team to push through openings while defenders react

atk.playstyle: Ace is a reliable hard breacher with remote, versatile breaching via S.E.L.M.A. He excels at creating multiple large breaches from safe positions, allowing flexible site entry and split-vector attacks. His 2 speed / 2 armor balance makes him a solid front-liner — not a pure anchor, but responsive enough to adapt to roamer threats. Ace plays best as a dedicated hard breacher who pre-plans breach vectors and coordinates team timing around S.E.L.M.A. deployment. His ability to breach walls, hatches, and destroy gadgets simultaneously makes him a Swiss Army knife operator. He is less vulnerable to roamer aggression than slower hard breachers (e.g., Thermite) due to his 2 speed, allowing mid-round repositioning if breaches fail.
strengths: Throws 3 S.E.L.M.A. charges per round — redundancy and flexibility
Creates dual openings on walls with clean rectangular geometry
Hydraulic breaching works on walls, hatches, floors, ceilings — extremely versatile
Can breach from range, staying out of defender sight lines
Extendable arms destroy gadgets during expansion — clears Frost Traps, Gu Mines, etc.
2 speed / 2 armor — balanced stat profile, responsive to roamer pressure
Destroys Mira Black Mirrors if caught in explosion radius
weaknesses: Bandit / Kaid — electrified walls prevent S.E.L.M.A. activation completely
Jäger / Wamai — can intercept S.E.L.M.A. during flight
Limited to 2 sequential detonations per device — some walls may require full breach timing
Hatches require 2 full charges to destroy (expensive on ammo-limited rounds)
Loud activation alerts defenders to breach location
S.E.L.M.A. cannot be recalled once thrown — failed throws are permanent
No protection from teammate fire during deployment phase

atk.name: Hibana

gadget.name: X-KAIROS Pellet Launcher
gadget.description: Deploys explosive gel pellets remotely detonated to breach reinforced walls. Hibana carries 18 total pellets per round with no reload requirement (Y10S4 change).
mechanics: 18 total pellets per round deployable in clusters
No reload required between deployments (major Y10S4 buff)
Pellets adhere to reinforced walls, soft surfaces, and various obstacles
Can create varied breach sizes depending on pellet clustering
Remote detonation allows flexible timing
Each pellet detonation creates blast radius effect
Cluster pellets for larger openings; spread for multiple small breaches
Destroys pellets upon detonation—must redeploy for additional breaches
tips: Deploy pellets before committing team to breach area
Use remote detonation to time breaches with team coordination
Coordinate with Thatcher EMP to clear electricity and enable safe detonation
Prioritize all 18 pellets strategically throughout round
Deploy in unpredictable patterns to avoid defender prediction
Use to destroy defender gadgets blocking key breach points

secondaries.notes: Full-auto secondary option; preferred over pistol per coaching rec

atk.playstyle: Hibana is a remote hard breacher who excels at flexible, reactive wall destruction. Unlike Thermite, who commits to immediate detonation, Hibana can place pellets and detonate when conditions are optimal. Her high speed (3) allows aggressive repositioning after pellet deployment, making her a hybrid hard breacher-fragger capable of both utility execution and combat pressure. Playing Hibana effectively requires understanding breach timing and map geometry. She should deploy pellets in strategic positions, retreat to safe locations, and coordinate detonation with team utility and positioning. Hibana is strongest when defenders cannot deny her pellet placements (through Thatcher EMP or positioning safety). She teaches flexible utility usage and reactive playstyle. Hibana's value scales with team coordination and map knowledge. On coordinated teams, her remote detonation enables synchronized site entry. On casual teams, her pellets may be wasted or detonated suboptimally. Her high speed makes her valuable for aggressive plays where hard breachers must also frag.
strengths: Remote detonation allows flexible, reactive breaching (major tactical advantage)
18 total pellets (Y10S4 buff) provides substantial breach capacity without reload
High speed (3) enables aggressive repositioning after deployment
Able to frag while providing hard breach utility
Can create varied breach sizes based on tactical situation
TYPE-89 is strong primary weapon with solid damage and handling
Flexible secondary gadgets enable utility adaptation
weaknesses: Slow pellet deployment animation leaves Hibana vulnerable during placement
Low armor (1) means minimal survivability in sustained combat
Remote detonation requires signal to detonated area
Predictable placement locations—experienced defenders counter-position
Pellet destruction interrupts breach timing—must redeploy if denied
Ineffective against Mute jammer radius (prevents detonation)
Mute Signal Disruptors can jam pellet detonation completely

atk.name: Maverick

gadget.name: Exothermic-S "Suri" Torch
gadget.description: Exothermic-S "Suri" Breaching Torch — A DIY-inspired blowtorch that silently burns precise holes through reinforced walls, hatches, and surfaces. The torch is almost inaudible, enabling silent utility denial and breach setup without alerting defenders.
mechanics: Torch fires a constant jet of flame with 2-meter maximum range
Destroys breakable surfaces instantly on contact
Reinforced surfaces require sustained flame focus for approximately 0.25 seconds to breach
Maverick carries 2 total torches per round
Each torch provides 13 seconds of fuel per tank (two tanks per torch = 26 seconds total per torch)
Fuel depletes while firing regardless of what the flame contacts
Reloading takes approximately 1 second; leftover fuel in a partially-used tank is lost on reload
Flame deals 5 damage to operators per 0.25 seconds (20 DPS to living targets)
tips: Burn silent holes through reinforced walls to destroy gadgets without alerting site (Jäger ADS, Maestro cameras, Wamai Mag-NET)
Coordinate with hard breachers (Ace, Thermite); burn gadgets blocking their approach while they prepare main entry
Use 3-speed advantage to reposition after burning gadgets—avoid defender counterattack
Create off-angle walls with burned holes for flanking shots during site execution
Prioritize gadgets that block hard breach entry (Jäger ADS, Wamai Mag-NET) over lower-impact gadgets
Save torch fuel carefully; calculate burn distance before committing

primaries.notes: Primary AR with good accuracy and controlled recoil

atk.playstyle: Maverick is a high-speed (3 Armor) anti-gadget operator who excels at pre-site gadget denial and setting up unconventional breach angles. Unlike Thermite or Ace, who create fast, loud entry points, Maverick plays a slower, methodical game of denying defender utility and opening sightlines without alerting the site. His 3-speed rating enables rapid repositioning after burning gadgets, allowing him to escape defender counterattacks and reposition for additional utility denial. Maverick is strongest when he has clear angles into the site or onto defender utility that needs destruction. He pairs excellently with hard breachers (Ace, Thermite) by pre-clearing their approach of utility before they deploy—his silent breaching enables team coordination without giving away entry timing. In round structure, Maverick typically plays mid-round or pre-site, buying time and denying gadgets while the team sets up. He can also play solo on undefended walls to create new angles, unconventional sightlines, and flanking routes that surprise defenders. His weakness is that he cannot create large breaches quickly, making him a supporting breacher rather than a primary hard breacher. Maps with few gadgets to target, heavy defender utility density, or aggressive defender positioning can limit his impact. Teams must give Maverick time to work, which can slow down aggressive attacking timelines. Bandit and Kaid electrification can interrupt torch fuel consumption.
strengths: Completely silent gadget denial—defenders cannot hear torch activation or operation
Can destroy gadgets through surfaces without breaching walls, maintaining team cover
3-speed enables rapid repositioning after burning gadgets, escaping counterattacks
Flexible role: works on site denial, roam denial, or as utility opener for hard breachers
Small burned holes enable off-angle picks before main site push
Torch is not affected by electrical defenses (Bandit, Kaid cannot interrupt)
Fuel efficiency enables multiple gadget destructions per torch with careful planning
weaknesses: Cannot hard breach quickly compared to Thermite, Ace, or Hibana
Limited fuel per torch requires careful gadget-target prioritization
Slow breach pace can be punished by aggressive roamer aggression
Requires good map knowledge and gadget placement understanding for effective play
Slow overall entry compared to fast attackers (Ash, Sledge, Recruit entry)
Gadget-focused role provides no value against gadget-light defenses
Holstering weapons to use torch creates vulnerability to immediate defender counterattack

atk.name: Thermite

gadget.name: Exothermic Charges
gadget.description: Thermite deploys exothermic charges on reinforced walls that burn through steel after detonation. He carries 3 charges per round. Charges cannot be countered or destroyed during the burn—once placed and activated, the breach is guaranteed and permanent. This makes him the most reliable hard breacher in the game.
mechanics: 3 exothermic charges per round (no recharge)
Burns through reinforced walls completely, creating sight line
Requires placement on wall and detonation activation
Audible loud detonation alerts defenders to breach location and timing
Cannot be destroyed, countered, or interrupted mid-burn by defenders
Permanent breach—no gadget can reverse or close the opening
Must place charge on reinforced wall surface
Brief setup/placement animation exposes Thermite
tips: Place charges before committing team to breach—allow burn time
Coordinate with Thatcher EMP first to clear Bandit/Kaid electrical denial
Position team to exploit breach opening immediately—expect defender aggression
Use all 3 charges strategically—prioritize key walls over redundant breaches
Call out placement and detonation timing to team—critical for coordination
Back off during burn to avoid feedback damage or defender counterfire

atk.playstyle: Thermite is the archetypal hard breacher and foundational operator for virtually every coordinated attack. His role is straightforward: place charges on reinforced walls, protect those charges during the burn, and establish control of the breached area. He is not an entry fragger but rather a setup operator who enables the entire team's site execution. Playing Thermite requires positioning discipline—he must place charges safely, back off during the burn, and be ready to hold the breach against defensive countermeasures. He benefits tremendously from utility support (Thatcher's EMP, IQ clearing gadgets, Twitch destroying cameras). On well-coordinated teams, Thermite enables predictable, methodical site takes. On disorganized teams, he can be isolated and eliminated while exposing his breach lines. Thermite's impact scales with team coordination and map knowledge. On sites like Bank, Consulate, or Coastline where key walls are contested, a strong Thermite player creates the entire attack foundation. The Y10S4 buff to 3 charges significantly improved his flexibility—previously, having only 2 charges forced difficult decisions about which walls to prioritize.
strengths: 3 charges per round (Y10S4 buff) provides flexibility for multiple breach options
Exothermic charges are the most reliable hard breach tool in the game
Charge placement can force defenders into reactive positions
2-2 stats provide balanced survivability and mobility
Strong primary weapons (556xi is reliable; M1014 enables CQB)
Irreplaceable for site execution on heavily fortified positions
weaknesses: Loud detonation alerts defenders to exact breach location and timing
Vulnerable during charge placement—requires cover and team support
Cannot rush after breach—committed playstyle leaves him exposed if team doesn't follow
Ineffective against sites with multiple hard walls or excessive defender utility
Bandit/Kaid can deny breach attempts if Thatcher support is unavailable
Secondary gadgets (grenades) don't provide hard-counter utility

atk.name: Buck

gadget.name: Skeleton Key (Pump-Action Shotgun Underbarrel)
gadget.description: Skeleton Key — a pump-action shotgun attachment mounted underbarrel on Buck's primary rifle. The weapon attachment allows Buck to create holes in soft surfaces (walls, ceilings, floors) without switching weapons or deploying separate gadgets. Unlimited usage throughout round.
mechanics: Integrated underbarrel shotgun on primary rifle (C8-SFW)
Creates rectangular holes in soft surfaces
Valid targets: soft walls, wooden hatches, wooden ceilings, destructible floors
Cannot breach reinforced walls, reinforced hatches, or electrified surfaces
Rounds are unlimited — no gadget consumption
Enables creating murder holes through floors for sightline control
Can breach horizontal surfaces (ceilings) to create elevation advantages
Enables rapid floor-to-floor breaching for vertical map control
tips: Identify vertical play opportunities before committing breaches
Create murder holes in ceilings to control upper/lower level sightlines
Breach soft walls rapidly to create multiple entry points
Coordinate vertical breaches with team — synergizes with floor/ceiling control
Save vertical plays for high-impact angles, not spam breaching
Use rifle between breaches for suppressive fire

atk.playstyle: Buck is a flexible soft breacher and vertical player who excels at creating complex, multi-directional breach options. Unlike Sledge (hammer-focused) or Ash (limited rounds), Buck's Skeleton Key is integrated into his primary weapon, allowing unlimited breaching throughout the round. He thrives on maps with vertical play opportunities (ceilings, hatches, multiple levels) where his murder hole playstyle dominates. Buck plays best as an aggressive vertical controller who creates elevation advantages through ceiling breaches and floor-to-floor play. His balanced 2-2 stats allow him to hold positions after creating vertical dominance. He is strongest on multi-level maps where vertical control translates to map pressure.
strengths: Skeleton Key allows unlimited breaching — integrated into primary weapon, no gadget limits
Enables rapid, aggressive vertical play through floors and ceilings
Murder hole creation forces defenders to vacate key positions
2-2 stats provide balanced survivability and mobility
Strong primary weapon options (C8-SFW reliable; CAMRS for ranged play)
Secondary gadgets provide flexibility (stuns for entry, claymore for roamer denial)
Can destroy gadgets without switching weapons
weaknesses: Skeleton Key breaches are loud — defenders anticipate and counter-position
Vertical play requires map knowledge — ineffective without understanding sightlines
Limited breaching power on flat maps (Bank, Coastline) where vertical vulnerabilities are fewer
Loud breaches prevent surprise element — defenders prepare for rushes
Ineffective against electrified surfaces (Bandit, Kaid)
Secondary gadgets lack hard-counter utility (no Thatcher equivalent)
CAMRS DMR less effective than dedicated sniper weapons

atk.name: Flores

profile.role: Soft Breach, Disable, Area Denial, Back Line

gadget.name: RCE-RATERO Charge
gadget.description: RCE-RATERO Drone — A remote-controlled explosive drone with 10-second timer. Flores carries 4 charges per round (plus 1 regular drone in prep phase, 1 additional during action phase).
mechanics: Remotely piloted like a standard drone with video feed
Can jump like a standard drone (3-second jump cooldown)
Automatically moves forward once Flores enters its feed
CANNOT reverse or stop in place—movement is forward-only while active
Can fit through drone holes and onto environmental props
Flores is vulnerable/stationary while piloting
10-second automatic timer—drone auto-anchors and detonates after timer expires
Manual detonation: pressing gadget button triggers bulletproof armor state (activates instantly)
tips: Scout first: use video feed to identify gadget locations and defender positions before committing
Use drones to clear gadgets blocking hard breach sites (Bandit, Kaid shock, Castle panels)
Park drone near high-value gadgets and detonate to deny area
Coordinate with hard breachers—drone removes utility before they push reinforced walls
Save drones for critical moments; don't waste on utility-light sites
Be aware of Mute range—jammed drones auto-detonate when Mute is destroyed

atk.playstyle: Flores is a versatile intel and area-denial operator who uses explosive drones to gather information and destroy defender utility. His 2-speed/2-armor profile provides balanced survivability and mobility. Flores is played as a mid-round support attacker, using drones to scout areas safely and remove key defensive gadgets before the team executes site breach. Flores excels at identifying roamer positions and gadget placements without directly engaging defenders. His drones provide flexibility—they can be used purely for intel gathering via video feed or detonated to destroy gadgets and area-deny positions. He is particularly strong against gadget-heavy defenses where multiple drones can systematically remove utility. His weakness is that drones are slow and vulnerable to destruction. Defenders who actively hunt drones can eliminate them quickly, limiting his gadget-denial capability. Flores is vulnerable while piloting drones, making him an easy target for aggressive roamers. Teams must protect Flores during drone piloting phases.
strengths: Flexible drones provide both intel gathering and gadget destruction
Video feed enables safe scouting without risking team members
4 drones enable multiple gadget targets or redundant intel coverage
Explosion damage applies area denial beyond gadget destruction
Can create new angles by breaking unreinforced walls
Good primary weapon options (AR33, SR-25, T-95 LSW)
2-speed/2-armor balances mobility and durability
weaknesses: Drones are slow and visible—easily destroyed by active defender hunting
Limited range and movement compared to faster drone operators
Flores is stationary and vulnerable while piloting drones
Slow drone movement can waste critical round time in fast-paced situations
Counterable by aggressive roamer attacks during drone deployment
Mute's Signal Disruptor completely jams drones in range
Limited effectiveness against roamer-heavy defenses

atk.name: Fuze

gadget.name: APM-6 "Matryoshka" Cluster Charge
gadget.description: Cluster Charges that deploy on soft walls, floors, ceilings, and hatches. Fuze carries 4 charges per round.
mechanics: Attaches to any destructible or reinforced surface
2-second deployment time (Fuze can be killed during deployment, charge is removed upon death)
One charge can be active at a time—deploying another immediately detonates the first
Reinforced surfaces take longer to activate after deployment
Remotely activated (cannot activate if Fuze is in DBNO state)
Fires 5 sub-grenades in a line (right to left), detonating in launch order
Each sub-grenade has 4.2-meter explosion radius
Lethal damage: Light operators up to 2m, Medium/Heavy operators up to 1.2m
tips: NEVER use in Hostage mode—grenades kill civilians, guaranteeing round loss
Use on floors/ceilings above defender positions for maximum chaos
Coordinate placement with team to avoid friendly fire
Three charges enable denial of multiple room entries or layered defense pressure
Loud detonation alerts defenders; follow up aggressively
Expect defenders to anticipate placement—use unpredictable positions

atk.playstyle: Fuze is an area denial operator whose primary strength is forcing defenders out of clustered positions through heavy explosive pressure. His extremely slow speed (1) and high armor (3) mean he is NOT a fragger—he is a methodical pusher who plants charges and advances behind their impact. Fuze excels on sites where defenders stack closely (objective rooms, corridor bottlenecks) where cluster grenades are devastating. Playing Fuze requires timing and map knowledge. He must position himself safely while placing charges, then advance into the area cleared by detonation. Fuze is at his strongest when defenders are predictably positioned and when area denial can control the entire round. He is weak when facing mobile, spread-out defenders who can adapt to his predictable playstyle. His extreme slowness makes him vulnerable to roamers and flankers. Teams must provide flank security. Fuze teaches aggressive area denial and methodical site execution—on tight, objective-focused sites, he can single-handedly force defensive breakdowns.
strengths: 4 cluster charges provide powerful area denial and damage output
Charges force defenders to reposition or take damage—creates openings for team
High armor (3) provides durability for aggressive pushing
LMG option provides excellent sustained damage capability
Charges can deny multiple room entries sequentially
Excellent for objective room clearing and site pressure
Ballistic Shield absorbs 66% explosive damage
weaknesses: Extremely slow (Speed 1)—vulnerable during repositioning and flank attacks
Charges are indiscriminate—kills teammates, attackers, and hostages (disastrous in Hostage mode)
Predictable playstyle—defenders anticipate charge locations and avoid
Loud detonation alerts defenders; no element of surprise after activation
Limited decision-making beyond placement
Ineffective against spread-out, mobile defender positioning
Vulnerability to roamers who surprise him from unexpected angles

atk.name: Ram

gadget.name: BU-GI Auto-Breacher
gadget.description: BU-GI Auto-Breacher — An automated, bulletproof mechanical drone equipped with cutting blades that destroys destructible walls, flooring, and gadgets along a set path. Activated remotely, the BU-GI clears vertical breach routes enabling floor destruction and overhead attacks. Ram carries 3 BU-GI drones per round.
mechanics: Ram carries 3 BU-GI drones per round (throwable gadgets)
Automatically activates and moves forward destroying all destructible surfaces in path
Can be set to travel: straight forward, swerve left, or swerve right
Instantly destroys barricades on contact
Makes loud mechanical noise during operation
Disables Aruni Surya Gate without being destroyed
Destroys all breakable walls and flooring instantly
Destroys destructible gadgets in path (Alibi Prismas, Maestro Evil Eyes, Lesion Gu Mines, etc.)
tips: Deploy BU-GI to create vertical breach holes in floors/ceilings
Use for accessing upper/lower levels without direct entry point exposure
Pair with teammates to execute through cleared paths immediately
Set direction carefully—drone follows determined path automatically
Protect drone from C4 and grenades during operation
Use BU-GI loud noise to mask defuser deployment or other breaching sounds

atk.playstyle: Ram is a heavy (3 Armor), slow (1 Speed) vertical breacher who enables unconventional attack vectors through automated floor destruction. Her BU-GI drones create overhead advantages by clearing ceilings and floors, enabling team entry from above—a mechanic unique to Ram. She is played as a support breacher complementing hard breachers with vertical access options. Ram shines on maps with strong vertical components (multi-floor objectives, accessible rafters, stacked rooms) where floor destruction creates tactical advantages and forces defender repositioning. Her drones force defenders to adapt positioning and create openings for coordinated team execution. She is particularly valuable against gadget-heavy defenses where the BU-GI destroys utility while creating new attack angles. Her weakness is 1-speed limiting repositioning and vulnerability to roamer aggression during drone setup. BU-GI drones can be destroyed by explosives or shot (rear canister) before completing breaches. She lacks offensive capability beyond vertical breaching—requires team follow-up to maximize value from cleared routes.
strengths: Automated drones enable vertical breaching without direct operator exposure
Creates overhead attack routes defenders don't anticipate or pre-aim
BU-GI destroys gadgets and obstacles along breaching path
Loud operation can strategically mask defuser deployment and other utility
High armor (3) provides durability in close-quarters roamer engagements
Works on all floor types destroying complete destructible surfaces
3 drones per round enable multiple breach attempts or locations
weaknesses: 1 speed creates severe vulnerability to roamer aggression
BU-GI easily destroyed by C4, grenades, or rear canister shots
Mute Signal Jammers prevent initial activation (active BU-GI continues unaffected)
Loud operation alerts defenders to exact breach location
Requires team coordination to execute through cleared paths
Limited offensive capability—support breacher only
Red canister is easily targetable—defenders can destroy drone mid-operation

atk.name: Sledge

gadget.name: Tactical Breaching Hammer "The Caber"
gadget.description: Sledge wields "The Caber," a specialized tactical breaching hammer built from differential heat-treated 4140 steel with a fiberglass grip. The hammer can breach destructible surfaces with relative silence, creating a rectangular hole. It has limited durability of 25 charges shown by a meter above the gadget icon.
mechanics: Breaching a wall = 2 charges
Breaching a barricade = 1 charge
Hitting environmental props = 1 charge
Destroying gadgets/killing enemies = 2 charges
Soft walls, barricades, and hatches—creates rectangular hole
Destroys bulletproof gadgets: Barbed Wire, Deployable Shields, Castle Armor Panels, Azami's Kiba Barriers, Bulletproof Cameras, Maestro's Evil Eyes
Cannot break reinforced walls (only consumes durability)
Cannot break non-destructible surfaces
tips: Manage durability carefully—25 charges limit total breach count per round
Use wall breaches (2 charges) strategically; barricade/gadget destruction (1 charge) is more efficient
Breach within 1 meter to ensure full wall penetration
Coordinate breaches with team to avoid defenders pre-positioning
Combine with Thatcher/IQ to clear gadgets before hammer entry

atk.playstyle: Sledge is a foundational soft breacher and entry operator who creates silent breach routes without alerting defenders. His 2-2 profile provides balanced mobility and survivability, allowing him to push aggressively after opening walls. His hammer creates flexible entry points across the map, forcing defenders to spread thin or abandon positions. Sledge excels at creating flanking routes and opening multiple soft walls to fracture defender coverage. Unlike explosive breachers, he generates no audio alert—ideal for sites where noise discipline is critical. His 25-charge durability requires careful planning; teams should identify key breach points before committing the hammer to suboptimal targets. He pairs exceptionally well with utility-clear operators (Thatcher, IQ) who neutralize gadget threats before he swings the hammer. His M590A1 or L85A2 enable follow-up gunfighting immediately after breaching.
strengths: Silent breaches with no audio alert to defenders
25-charge durability allows multiple breach routes per round
Balanced 2-2 stats provide durability without sacrificing mobility
Destroys many defender gadgets (shields, cameras, barbed wire, etc.)
Creates flanking routes that force defenders to reposition
Hammer can melee-kill through shields (Clash) without detonating them
weaknesses: Requires close proximity to breach—vulnerable during swing animation
Charge consumption is limited—poor resource management wastes utility
Predictable breach points—experienced defenders listen and pre-position
Shotgun has limited range for long-sight engagements post-breach
Impact EMP option prevents gadget destruction but lacks concussive utility
No hard counter utility (grenades are area-denial, not direct denial)

atk.name: Brava

gadget.name: Kludge Drone
gadget.description: Kludge Drone — a small remote-controlled drone that hacks defender gadgets on contact, converting them to provide attacker utility. The drone maintains the gadget's original function but reverses the operational benefit.
mechanics: Brava carries 2 Kludge drones per round
Drones are small and maneuverable, difficult to visually track
Contact with any defender gadget automatically initiates hack
Hacked gadgets remain converted for remainder of round
Each drone can hack ONE gadget, then is consumed
Jäger ADS — Destroys attacker grenades → Destroys defender grenades instead
Wamai MAG-NET — Intercepts attacker projectiles → Intercepts defender projectiles
Maestro Cameras — Provides defender surveillance → Provides attacker surveillance
tips: Scout gadget locations before committing drone (use intel operator support)
Prioritize high-impact gadgets (Jäger ADS, Wamai MAG-NET) for conversion
Pilot drones carefully to avoid early detection by defenders
Use converted gadgets actively to deny defenders utility throughout round
Coordinate with team to exploit hacked gadgets (e.g., grenade denial flipped to defend attackers)
Save drones for critical gadgets rather than wasting on low-impact targets

atk.playstyle: Brava is a high-speed (3), low-armor (1) gadget-conversion specialist who turns defender utility into attacker advantage. Her playstyle is tech-focused — she deploys drones, hacks critical gadgets, and enables team execution through converted utility. Brava is played as a mid-round or utility-denial operator who systematically removes defender gadget advantages. Brava excels against gadget-heavy defenses where converting key utilities provides massive strategic advantage. Her ability to flip Jäger ADS or Wamai MAG-NET from defense to offense is particularly powerful on maps where these gadgets create dense coverage (Kafe, Bank, Coastline). She is particularly strong in post-plant scenarios where converted ADS can protect the defuser site.
strengths: Gadget conversion flips defender utility into attacker advantage
Hacked Jäger ADS and Wamai MAG-NET provide massive strategic advantage (grenades now benefit attackers)
2 drones enable multiple gadget targets or redundant hacking attempts
Drones are small and difficult to visually track
3 speed allows rapid repositioning after drone deployment
Hacked gadgets provide ongoing utility throughout round
Good primary weapons (PARA-308, ARX200) for backup gunfighting
weaknesses: Very low armor (1) — vulnerable in direct engagements
Drones are destroyable — defenders can eliminate before hacking occurs
Limited to 2 drones per round — restricts gadget-hacking flexibility
Brava is stationary while piloting — vulnerable to roamer flanks during drone operation
Not all gadgets are hackable — explosives and melee gadgets cannot be converted
Ineffective against gadget-light defenses — roamer-focused lineups negate her utility
Montagne and other shield operators counter Brava effectively

atk.name: Kali

gadget.name: LV Explosive Lance
gadget.description: LV Explosive Lance — An under-barrel mounted launcher firing specialized explosive projectiles that burrow through breakable and reinforced walls, floors, and ceilings, detonating on the opposite side to destroy defender gadgets without breaching the surface.
mechanics: Kali carries 4 LV Lances per round
Lances fire in a straight line with no projectile arc—travel indefinitely until hitting a surface
Burrowing through walls takes approximately 3 seconds to complete
Projectiles have visible size and particle effects during burrowing
Lances can be destroyed by gunfire while burrowing; reinforced walls can only be damaged at the lance's tail-end (not the burrowing tip)
Lances are electrically resistant—not destroyed by Bandit Shock Wires or Kaid Electroclaws
Upon completing burrowing, detonates on BOTH sides of the wall with equal 3-meter radius explosions
Damage drops off with distance; some gadgets may survive if positioned far from detonation center
tips: Scout gadget positions with drones before committing lances
Identify key defensive gadgets (Maestro cameras above/below, ADS covering site, Wamai Mag-NET placement)
Coordinate lance angles with hard breachers (Thermite, Hibana, Ace) to clear defensive utility
Use vertical angles to destroy gadgets in unexpected locations (cameras on upper floors, gadgets in rafters)
Pre-fire lances through predicted gadget locations to deny utility setup
Focus on high-value gadget targets (Jäger ADS, Black Mirror Mira) over low-impact gadgets

primaries.notes: Powerful semi-auto sniper; can breach barricades and hatches in single shot
Fires LV Explosive Lances; primary gadget delivery mechanism

atk.playstyle: Kali is a specialized anti-gadget operator who uses vertical and horizontal utility denial to create safe attack vectors. Her balanced 2-2 stats enable flexibility, but her playstyle is methodical and deliberate. She identifies gadget positions through drone intel, sets up firing sightlines, and executes lance shots from protected positions. Kali is played as a mid-round or support attacker, focusing on gadget denial rather than direct site entry. Kali excels on maps with predictable vertical gadget placement (cameras on upper floors, ADS on rafters, Maestro cameras covering from above) and in coordinated team setups where drone intel confirms gadget locations. Her unique vertical destruction mechanic enables denying defensive utility without exposing attacker positions. She synergizes excellently with hard breachers who can execute clean site breaches once gadget interference is removed. Her weakness is the slow, deliberate pace required to identify targets, set up sightlines, and fire lances. Against aggressive defenders or under time pressure, Kali's setup time becomes a critical liability. She also requires extensive map knowledge and drone intel to function effectively—less effective in blind map picks, dynamic defensive setups, or against gadget-light defenses. The CSRX sniper rifle is also weaker in close-range engagements compared to assault rifles.

atk.playstyle: Thatcher is the premier hard counter to defender gadget setups. As an anti-gadget operator, he thrives in coordinated play where his EMPs are timed with teammate utilities. He is not an entry fragger but rather a setup operator who enables his team's breaches. Thatcher's value is determined entirely by the defenders' gadget setup—against gadget-heavy sites (Kaid walls, Bandit batteries, Jäger ADS clusters), he is essential; against gadget-light sites, his presence is less critical. Playing Thatcher requires game sense about defender utility locations and cooperation with your team. He should EMP key positions before hard breachers commit, creating windows of opportunity. His balanced 2-2 stats allow him to hold off-angles after utility consumption, but he is not a fragger—his role is pure utility execution. On coordinated teams, Thatcher is nearly mandatory for consistent site execution. His EMPs remove the defender's early-game gadget advantage, making subsequent breaches significantly safer. He teaches players the importance of team utility timing and preparation before aggressive plays.

Twitch excels on teams with strong individual fraggers who don't need heavy utility setup—her drones provide supplementary gadget denial and intel rather than guaranteed breach enablement. She is extremely valuable on maps with elevated Valkyrie camera positions, clustered Jäger ADS arrays, or multiple defender utility locations that benefit from distributed denial. Playing Twitch requires multitasking—drone piloting while maintaining map awareness. She teaches players the importance of intel gathering and adaptive utility usage. On coordinated teams, she synergizes exceptionally well with hard breachers when her drones handle gadget denial. On casual teams, her drones can be wasted without proper setup communication.

atk.playstyle: Gridlock is a low-speed (1), high-armor (3) area-denial specialist who controls territory through sustained thorn deployment. Her slow movement speed is offset by her ability to dictate how defenders navigate the map, forcing them into unfavorable rotations and congesting site entrances. Gridlock is played as a second-wave or support attacker, establishing denial zones after the hard breacher opens the site. Gridlock excels on maps with multiple roamer routes and open corridors where defenders naturally spread out. Her Trax forces defenders together, making them easier targets for coordinated team engagement. She is particularly strong on maps like Bank, Coastline, and Oregon where roaming is common. Gridlock's high armor allows her to survive close engagements and plant in denied areas that defenders cannot push through. Her weakness is mobility—1 speed makes her vulnerable to quick roamer attacks and limits her ability to adapt to aggressive defender counterplay. She is also weak against mobile defenders (Nomad, Capitão) who can outmaneuver her denial and create new attack vectors.

atk.playstyle: Montagne is a specialized support operator who excels at controlling space and creating safe passages for teammates. He is not a fragger but rather a mobile fortification that forces defenders to reposition or commit resources to eliminate him. His intentionally slow speed (1 Armor/1 Speed rating) is designed for anchoring positions and providing steady, methodical pressure. He is a team-dependent operator—his value multiplies when teammates understand how to leverage his shield coverage. Playing Montagne effectively requires patience, positioning discipline, and constant communication with teammates. He must position his shield to deny lines of sight while remaining aware of flanking routes and multi-angle threats. Montagne is strongest on maps with linear corridors and choke points where his shield can dominate access routes (e.g., narrow hallways on Bank, Coastline, Kanal). He is weak against grenades, C4, concussion effects, and multi-angle flanking—his vulnerability to explosives and simultaneous threats limits his application on open-concept sites. Montagne teaches team coordination and support-focused play. He thrives when teammates understand complementary roles: one teammate covers flanks, another enables hard breach execution, and communication prevents grenades from being thrown when shield is exposed. On highly coordinated teams, Montagne can single-handedly control defensive positions by forcing defenders into reactive, unfavorable angles.

atk.playstyle: Nomad is an area-denial operator who specializes in crowd control and disrupting defender counterattacks. Her balanced 2-2 stats enable flexible positioning and map mobility while maintaining durability. She is played as a site supporter and area controller rather than a hard breacher—she deploys Airjabs around the objective or near key flanking routes, then supports the team's main push, plant execution, and post-plant defense. Nomad shines when the attacking team wants to secure plant positions and prevent defender re-entry or aggressive counter-rotations. Her Airjab grenades force defenders to take alternate routes, commit resources to destroying grenades, or risk knockback damage and positional disruption. She is particularly valuable on maps with multiple roamer routes or confined site areas where defenders naturally counter-push (Bank, Oregon, Coastline). Nomad works well in teams that plant early and need to hold the plant against organized retakes. Her weakness is limited value if defenders never re-enter the area or if her grenades are destroyed before detonation. Against teams with strong objective holdouts, Nomad's mines provide little value. She is also limited by the 3-grenade cap per round—once grenades are destroyed or detonated, she has no additional area denial tools. Teams must have clear plant strategies and coordinate rotations for Nomad to maximize impact.
strengths: Flexible area denial—protects plant, flank routes, or attacker positions simultaneously

atk.playstyle: Osa is a high-armor (3), low-speed (1) plant-support operator who enables safe defuser placement through transparent protective shields. Her playstyle is methodical and deliberate—she positions herself strategically, deploys shields to create protected plant windows, and provides passive cover throughout the round. Osa is played as a late-round or plant-execution specialist, focused entirely on enabling safe planting and post-plant site defense. Osa shines in plant-centric team strategies where teams commit to early defuser placement and hold the position against organized defender retakes. Her transparent shields create tactical windows that allow the planting player to execute defuser placement (10-second animation) without taking distance damage from defender overwatch angles. She is particularly valuable on sites with open plant locations (Stadium Plant, Kafe Piano) where defenders have long-range sight lines, making unarmed plant execution dangerous. Her weakness is lack of offensive capability—shields only provide passive cover, not gadget denial, breach support, or entry denial. Her 1-speed rating severely limits repositioning and adaptation to aggressive roamer counter-attacks. She is also entirely dependent on team execution—shields provide value only if teammates are positioned to capitalize on the cover and execute plant safely.

Her playstyle revolves around understanding sight angles and strategically placing vision walls to funnel defenders or open push routes. The toggle mechanic allows adaptive play—activate projectors when pushing, deactivate when holding to conserve battery. Her lightweight build and strong primary weapons allow her to follow up on the vision denial she creates, but her low armor means she cannot tank damage. Teams using Sens should leverage the vision denial to create favorable engagements rather than expecting Sens to duel defenders directly.

def.playstyle: Bandit is a wall-control specialist who denies hard-breach attempts through wall electrification. His role is to prevent Thermite, Hibana, and Ace from breaching critical reinforced walls, forcing attackers to adapt their breach strategy or expend additional utility to clear Shock Wires first. Bandit operates at two distinct skill tiers: Tier 1 (Beginner): Deploy Shock Wires on predicted breach locations during setup phase and let them persist. No timing required. Moderate value—attackers can coordinate utility clear (Twitch + IQ) to breach anyway. Tier 2 (Intermediate/Advanced/Elite): Execute "Bandit tricks" by timing Shock Wire placement to destroy charges in real-time. Requires sound cue recognition and precise 2-second timing. Extremely high value—skilled Bandit players can make walls completely unkillable, forcing attackers to burn multiple utilities or abandon breach vectors entirely. Bandit's 3/1 speed/armor profile enables roaming between wall locations during mid-round, repositioning to flank attackers, or rotating to defend different site sections. He's strongest on maps with multiple reinforced walls requiring defense (Bank vault, Coastline master bedroom, Club House trophy room) and weakest on maps with few walls or fast wall destruction options. MP7 provides agile movement for roaming; M870 offers close-quarters pressure if caught near walls. Nitro Cell enables post-plant denial synergy with wall control.

def.playstyle: Mute is a heavy anchor specializing in electronic denial and breach prevention. His core function is denying attacker electronic gadgets through jammer placement. Unlike roamers, Mute remains site-proximal to protect his jammers and respond to destruction attempts. He excels on reinforced-wall-heavy sites where jammer placement near breach points provides maximum value. Mute's playstyle centers on early jammer setup near walls or objective site, followed by defensive anchoring. During setup phase, he places 4 jammers in strategic locations: some protecting walls from breach charges, others securing objective from drone/gadget infiltration. During attack phase, he holds near jammers and defends against destruction attempts. His 3-armor allows him to withstand damage while repositioning between jammer positions. Success depends on jammer placement accuracy, protecting jammers from early destruction, and coordinating wall defense with Bandit/Kaid for layered denial.
strengths: Breach Prevention: Jammers block Thermite, Hibana, and Ace charge detonation — direct counter to hard-breachers
Drone Denial: Multiple jammers create extensive no-fly zones for attacker reconnaissance
Flexible Coverage: Works against multiple attacker gadget types (Dokkaebi, Goyo, IQ, Gridlock, etc.)
Persistent Gadget: 4 jammers remain active all round; early placement provides all-game value
Wall Synergy: Jammers + Bandit electrification = walls become unkillable (denial + destruction prevention)
3-Armor Durability: Heavy armor allows holding defensive positions and protecting jammer areas
4 Placements: Four jammers allow coverage of multiple site areas or layered denial zones
weaknesses: Twitch Vulnerability: Twitch drones can systematically destroy all 4 jammers from range if unprotected
EMP Vulnerability: Thatcher's EMP disables jammers for 10 seconds (soft counter, not permanent)
Jammer Fragility: Small profile; once located, attackers can destroy with minimal utility
Placement Skill Floor: Optimal jammer placement requires deep map knowledge
Pre-Placement Requirement: Jammers must be set up before attackers arrive; aggressive early breach can bypass
Slow Movement: 1-speed limits repositioning and rotation
IQ Scanner Counter: IQ's Electronics Detector reveals all jammer locations through walls

def.name: Tubarao

gadget.name: Zoto Canister
gadget.description: Zoto Canister ×3 — throwable devices that create temporary frozen areas deactivating electronic/mechanical gadgets and slowing attackers. Canisters deactivate gadgets for 12 seconds and leave footprint trails of attackers who pass through.
mechanics: Deploy up to 3 canisters via throwing (impact detonation)
Canisters stick to surfaces and release freezing gas
Frozen area deactivates ALL electronic/mechanical gadgets in radius for 12 seconds
Gadgets affected: Thermite charges, Hibana pellets, drones, breach charges, gadget-based utilities
Also disables Defender gadgets (friendly fire effect)
Thawing time: exactly 12 seconds before gadgets reactivate
Attackers walking through frozen area are slowed
Frozen area leaves footprint trails of attacker passage
tips: Place canisters on common breach points and entry pathways
Position to freeze high-value breaching utility (Thermite, Hibana, Ace)
Coordinate placement with team to deny specific utility in sequence
Stack canisters to create overlapping 12-second denial zones
Use footprint tracking to locate vertical attackers above/below
Combine with gadget denial operators (Bandit, Kaid, Mute) for layered defense

def.playstyle: Tubarão is a balanced anti-entry operator designed to disrupt attacker utility timing through temporary gadget freezing. His playstyle combines gadget denial with area control and vertical tracking capabilities. Unlike traditional utility denial (destruction), Tubarão freezes gadgets for 12 seconds, forcing attackers to wait out the duration or destroy the canister. This creates timing pressure and decision-making tension. His 2-2 stats enable flexible positioning while maintaining gadget coverage. He excels against breacher-heavy attacks (Thermite, Hibana, Ace) by disrupting breach synchronization. The footprint mechanic provides unique vertical Intel, allowing him to track attackers above/below his canister placement. He plays best as a flexible defender who adapts canister placement based on expected attacker compositions.
strengths: Unique 12-second gadget deactivation mechanic (not destruction)
Footprint tracking reveals vertical attacker movement
Multiple canisters create overlapping denial windows
Excellent against synchronized breacher attacks
Disrupts attacker timing without destroying utility
Balanced 2-2 stats enable both area denial and fragging
Synergizes well with gadget denial operators and trap operators
weaknesses: Maverick Tactical Breaching Torch melts ice (direct counter)
Gadget deactivation is temporary (12 seconds only)
Explosions (grenades, Zofia KS-79) can destroy canisters
Freezing affects friendly gadgets equally (no discrimination)
Limited value if attackers wait out deactivation duration
Attackers can destroy canisters before deploying utility
Ying can disable goggles but becomes vulnerable to her own Candela

def.name: Caveira

gadget.name: Silent Step + Interrogation
gadget.description: **Gadget Setup:** Caveira's gadget is unique because it combines two integrated mechanics: **(1) Silent Step (Passive):** Caveira moves silently with zero audio signature at any movement speed (walking, running, sprinting). Attackers cannot hear her footsteps or position through audio cues. **(2) Interrogation (Active):** When Caveira interacts with a downed (DBNO) attacker, she interrogates them, forcing a confession that reveals the real-time position of ALL living attackers to her entire team for ~10 seconds.

primaries.notes: Sustained DPS, precision — **42 dmg** _(Y11S1.1 buff, was 40)_

def.playstyle: Caveira is an elite roaming operator who specializes in eliminating isolated attackers through ambush and extracting team-wide intelligence through interrogation. Her role is to hunt attackers in secondary/flanking positions, down them (usually through ambush advantage), and interrogate for complete attacker positioning. Caveira's playstyle is aggressive roaming focused on isolation. During early-round, she uses Silent Step to position herself on ambush routes before attackers arrive. When attackers move through her position, she engages from advantage (behind, close range, elevated) and attempts to down targets using melee or Luison headshots. If successful, she interrogates immediately, revealing all remaining attackers to her team. Her team then capitalizes on revealed positions to reposition, counter-attack, or prepare for incoming assault. Unlike other roamers who provide damage or trapping, Caveira provides unmatched intel through interrogation. Single successful interrogation can swing entire round momentum—her team gains full attacker positioning while attackers lose element of surprise. Caveira is strongest on maps with multiple roam positions and separated entry routes (Bank, Coastline, Chalet) where she can isolate attackers. She is weaker on maps with narrow corridors or coordinated attacker bunching where isolated targets are rare. Her success depends heavily on team follow-up: interrogation intel is worthless if teammates cannot capitalize on revealed positions.
strengths: Unique interrogation mechanic — only operator who reveals ALL enemy positions simultaneously; unmatched intel value
Silent movement advantage — Silent Step allows positioning without audio warning; ambush advantage is significant
Roaming flexibility (3 speed) — rapid repositioning across map; can hunt multiple attacker routes
Team intel multiplier — single interrogation provides entire team information; indirect value is enormous
Suppressed weapon — Luison is quiet; silent executions don't alert distant attackers to Caveira's position
DBNO guarantee on Luison — ensures downed targets for interrogation even with headshots
weaknesses: Extremely fragile (1 armor) — loses direct gunfights against prepared attackers; depends on ambush advantage
Requires downed targets — cannot interrogate already-dead attackers; must specifically down targets (not kill)
Interrogation interruptible — teammate revive or Caveira death stops interrogation execution
Team dependent — if teammates don't follow up on interrogation intel, value is wasted
Vulnerable while executing — during interrogation animation, Caveira is stationary and vulnerable to fire
Predictable after interrogation — after interrogating, Caveira's position often revealed through team reactions
Countered by: Dokkaebi (Logic Bomb calls interrupt execution voice line, though interrogation still triggers), Jackal (if Silent Step deactivated), attacker coordination preventing isolation

def.name: Clash

profile.role: Area Denial, Crowd Control, Entry Blocker

gadget.name: CCE Shield (Crowd Control Electro Shield)
gadget.description: Crowd Control Electro Shield (CCE Shield) — an extendable electrified riot shield that serves as Clash's primary weapon. When deployed, the shield covers her entire front body and generates a high-voltage Charged Field Generator (CFG) that emanates directly in front of the shield in a cylindrical shape. The CFG damages and slows any attacker within range.
mechanics: Full-body front protection when deployed
Extends to cover entire front-facing side
Cannot be carried like normal ballistic shields
Opaque barrier prevents visual contact through shield
Deployable on ground and remotely activated for cover
Only deployed form, not handheld carry option
Range: Cylindrical effect directly in front of shield (~12 meters, exact range still being confirmed per wiki)
Damage over time: Attackers in CFG contact sustain low continuous damage

def.playstyle: Clash is a unique defender designed to create temporary area denial and frustration through active blocking rather than passive traps. Her electrified riot shield forces attackers to either spend significant utility to eliminate her, find alternative routes, or commit to a dangerous melee assault. She plays as a roamer deterrent and entry blocker—her presence in a corridor slows and damages aggressive pushes, buying time for teammates to rotate and set up defensive positions. Her primary strength is psychological pressure and time denial. Attackers must account for her presence, spend utility (grenades, flashbangs, C4) to neutralize her, or find alternative routes entirely. When played correctly, Clash forces attackers into predictable patterns or burns resources early in the round. However, her slow movement speed (1 speed) and inability to deal consistent offensive damage make her vulnerable if isolated or caught away from teammates. Clash thrives in coordinated team play. She should never hold a position alone; instead, she works best with aggressive teammates who can punish distracted attackers while they face her shield. Her SPSMG9 secondary provides close-quarters burst damage when switching from shield mode, creating a shock-and-stun playstyle where teammates capitalize on Clash's defensive control.
strengths: Full-body protection — provides bulletproof cover and blocks line of sight
Charged Field Generator damage/slow — continuous damage to attackers in front of shield; slows aggressive pushes
Forces utility expenditure — attackers must use grenades, C4, or flashbangs to neutralize her
Psychological pressure — attackers fear shield positions; creates hesitation and predictable behavior
Excellent at denying roam catches — shield blocks aggressive attacker flanks; delays plant/defuse attempts
Explosive resistance (66%) — teammates behind shield also receive damage reduction
weaknesses: Limited offensive capability — cannot fire primary weapon while shield is active; switch to secondary for damage
Vulnerable to melee attacks — knife rushing, melee strikes knock shield aside
Vulnerable to stuns — flashbangs, concussions (Zofia/Ela), Kali knockback disable shield positioning temporarily
Slow movement (1 speed) limits rotation flexibility; cannot rapidly reposition if push fails
Easily countered by coordinated utility — C4, grenades, flashbangs from height/distance eliminate shield advantage
Stamina/energy management — shield cannot be held indefinitely; requires positioning discipline
Requires strong team support — isolated Clash is vulnerable to flanking attackers; needs teammates nearby for covering fire

def.name: Ela

gadget.name: Grzmot Mines (×3)
gadget.description: Ela deploys three concussion proximity mines (inspired by WWII Polish grenades). Each mine can be thrown and adheres to any surface (walls, ceilings, floors, furniture). Once armed, mines detonate when an attacker enters their proximity radius, disorienting all operators in the blast area.
mechanics: Ela throws mines to deploy; they stick to any surface
Visual "sticky bag" puffs up over time but is purely cosmetic — no need to wait before throwing
After sticking, mines take 1 second to arm
While arming, a visual radius indicator appears on-screen showing the mine's effective range
Once armed, mines are triggered by attacker proximity
Proximity Radius: 4 meters — mine triggers when any attacker enters this radius
Blast Radius: 6 meters — slightly larger than proximity radius; concussion effect applies within this range
Line of Sight Requirement: Mine requires line of sight to the attacker's upper torso to trigger; if blocked by walls or obstacles, mine will NOT detonate even if attacker is in proximity

primaries.notes: Primary SMG; excellent for aggressive roaming

def.playstyle: Ela is an aggressive roaming trapper who excels at disorienting attackers and capitalizing on concussion windows with aggressive gunfighting. Unlike passive trappers (Kapkan, Lesion, Frost), Ela is actively engaged with her gadget, positioning herself immediately adjacent to or behind concussion points to follow up with burst damage. Her 3-speed/1-armor profile enables rapid repositioning and aggressive roaming playstyle. Ela's strategy unfolds in two phases: (1) Setup Phase: Place 1-2 mines on anticipated roam paths; hold 1 mine for reactive deployment. (2) Action Phase: Hold positioned near mine locations; when attackers trigger mines, immediately capitalize on the concussion window with aggressive SMG fire. Her lightweight profile allows for flexible rotations and rapid repositioning to hunt disoriented targets. Ela thrives in coordinated, mechanically aggressive team compositions where burst damage and synchronized follow-up are guaranteed. She struggles in isolated roaming where she cannot guarantee teammate support or in uncoordinated teams where concussion advantage is wasted.
strengths: Action Phase Flexibility: Unlike traditional trappers, Ela can deploy mines reactively during combat — not limited to setup-phase placement
Severe Concussion Effect: Disorientation is immediate and complete (blindness, deafness, vision loss) — concussed attackers are extremely vulnerable for 5 seconds
Aggressive Roaming: 3-speed allows rapid repositioning and aggressive gunfighting; mines enable burst elimination combos
Detonation Intel: Mine activation provides audio and visual feedback — Ela knows attackers are present and positioned
Scorpion Synergy: SMG excels at sustained fire on concussed targets; mine + immediate gunfight creates high-damage combo
Reactive Pressure: Can throw mines at attacking force proactively to concuss and disrupt organized pushes
weaknesses: Fragile: 1-armor/3-speed loses direct gunfights without concussion advantage; extremely vulnerable if mines are exhausted
Requires Team Coordination: Concussed attackers can recover if teammates don't engage immediately; solo plays are high-risk
Electronic Vulnerability: IQ detects mines easily; Thatcher EMP disables all mines instantly; attackers can preemptively destroy mines
Limited Effective Range: Proximity/blast radius are localized; attackers outside 6 meters are unaffected
Skill Dependent: Success heavily dependent on gunfighting ability and mine placement timing; high skill floor
Exposed During Placement: Throwing mines during action phase makes Ela stationary and vulnerable
Line of Sight Requirement: Mine requires sight of attacker's upper torso to trigger — can be avoided by flanking or staying behind cover

def.name: Oryx

gadget.name: Remah Dash
gadget.description: Tactical wall-breaking dash ability and hatch vertical mobility. Oryx can charge through unreinforced walls in a powerful dash, vaulting into adjacent rooms. He can also pull/climb through hatches from below, providing vertical rotations unavailable to other operators.
mechanics: 4 dash charges per round; each dash requires one charge
Can only dash through unreinforced (soft) walls
Cannot dash through reinforced walls, Castle panels, metal surfaces, or barricades
Dashing propels Oryx forward quickly, covering distance in milliseconds
Dash creates audible noise detectable by nearby attackers (alerts team/enemy)
Dash momentum can push Oryx forward uncontrollably; brief movement commitment
Brief vulnerability window during and immediately after dash completion
Dashing into attackers can cause minor damage (unknown exact mechanics)
tips: Pre-plan dash routes to enable aggressive vertical rotations
Use dashes to rotate off-site and create catch opportunities
Reserve dashes for key moments: catches, escapes, rotation speed plays
Use hatches repeatedly and silently for stealth rotations
Coordinate dash timings with teammates to create multi-angle attacks
Dash aggressively into engagements or retreat through walls to confuse attackers

def.playstyle: Oryx is a balanced-stat roamer specializing in vertical mobility and aggressive repositioning through wall dashing and hatch climbing. His playstyle centers on creating unpredictable rotation paths, forcing attackers to account for unexpected entry angles. Unlike traditional roamers, Oryx's dash ability provides instant wall traversals, enabling rapid vertical control. Oryx excels on multi-floor maps with strong hatch connectivity (Villa, Bank, Consulate). His ability to climb hatches from below and dash through soft walls enables escape routes and entry points unavailable to other operators. His 2-2 stats allow flexible anchor or roaming roles depending on team composition and site demands. Success depends on map knowledge, dash route planning, and team coordination around unexpected entry angles. He is most effective when coordinating with teammates—dashes can be used to bait attacker responses or create multi-angle attacks. Aggressive positioning and rotational speed are his core strengths.
strengths: Unique Vertical Mobility: Wall dashing and hatch climbing provide unmatched rotation speed
Unpredictable Rotations: Dash routes create entry angles attackers must account for
Rapid Repositioning: Dash allows escaping collapsing positions instantly
Hatch Control: Unlimited hatch climbs provide vertical escape/reentry options
Balanced Stats: 2-2 profile allows flexible anchor or roaming roles
Aggressive Potential: Quick rotations enable catch plays and multi-angle attacks
Escape Options: Dash and hatches provide multiple escape routes when compromised
weaknesses: Noise Creation: Dashing creates audible alert; eliminates surprise if misused
Limited Dashes: 4 dashes per round require strategic usage; cannot spam
Wall Limitations: Cannot dash reinforced walls, Castle panels, or metal surfaces
Vulnerability: Brief vulnerability window during/after dashing
Map Dependent: Less effective on maps with sparse soft walls or poor hatch connectivity
Skill Floor: Requires strong map knowledge to use dashes effectively
No Static Defense: Not ideal for passive anchor play; aggressive roaming only

def.name: Skopos

gadget.name: V10 Pantheon Shells
gadget.description: V10 Pantheon Shells — a pair of humanoid robots named Colossus and Talos which Skopós remotely pilots. She can only actively control one Shell at a time. The other Shell enters an inactive state, crouching and deploying a Bulletproof Shield while acting as a remote camera.
mechanics: Acts as the operator — can move, shoot, vault, and use gadgets like any defender
Can enter Observation Tool mode
If defeated, Skopós is eliminated from the round
Headshots: instant destruction depends on caliber (e.g. D-50 instantly destroys; 5.7 USG does not)
Treated as a gadget, not an operator — destroying it does NOT eliminate Skopós
Crouches and deploys a Bulletproof Shield with bulletproof glass at head height for observation
Shield glass can be shattered (vision blocked but protection remains)
Skopós is alerted when Inactive Shell is attacked (e.g. Brava's Kludge Drone)
tips: Use the Inactive Shell as a roving camera — park it with line of sight to a doorway and switch to it mid-round for free intel
The Inactive Shell's shield can block a doorway or corridor as a barricade while you play the other shell elsewhere
If you're being tracked (Grim Hive, Deimos Deathmark, Jackal Eyenox), switch to your other shell — the tracking resets
Manage ammo carefully — two shells share secondary gadgets, so don't burn both Impact Grenades early
EMP is your biggest counter — play cautiously in rounds with Thatcher; lose OT access and can't swap shells

def.playstyle: Skopós is a unique two-body intel and support operator. She provides unparalleled map coverage — effectively occupying two positions simultaneously. Her Inactive Shell is a persistent camera that can contest space and she can pivot to it at any moment. Her Active Shell plays like any defender but with the significant caveat that she's mechanically vulnerable in ways human operators are not. She plays best as a roaming support — placing her Inactive Shell to watch a flank or track a push while her Active Shell rotates or holds an angle. In post-plant scenarios her second body can contest a defuse from a different angle than her active position.
strengths: Simultaneously covers two positions — exceptional split-map pressure
Immune to gas (Smoke + Fenrir counters don't apply)
Intel advantage throughout the round via Inactive Shell camera
Can escape gadget tracking (Grim, Deimos, Jackal) by switching shells
Inactive Shell acts as a barricade/shield in tight corridors
Switching shells allows rapid repositioning with no rotation time
weaknesses: EMP (Thatcher, Impact EMP) disables shell switching — stranded in one body
Dokkaebi hacks Inactive Shell, locking Skopós out of it
Brava's Kludge Drone destroys Inactive Shell
IQ detects Inactive Shell at all times (persistent camera spotted easily)
Claymores placed against Inactive Shell detonate when Skopós activates it
Cannot be healed — no Doc, no Thunderbird, no Rook
Instant death on HP zero (no DBNO safety net)

def.name: Vigil

gadget.name: ERC-7 (Electronic Rendering Cloak)
gadget.description: ERC-7 Electronic Rendering Cloak — a backpack-mounted device using diminished reality technology that scans surrounding electronic devices and wipes Vigil's image from camera feeds and drone footage. Removes Vigil's visual presence from all attacker electronic surveillance with unlimited effective range.
mechanics: Maximum duration: 12 seconds per activation
Minimum charge required: 25% battery
Recharge rate: twice as fast as depletion rate (max 6 seconds recharge)
Can manually deactivate early to preserve charge
Takes 1 second to make Vigil fully invisible to cameras/drones
Infinite uses — can activate/deactivate multiple times per round
Removes Vigil from ALL attacker camera feeds and drones
Affects: drones, surveillance cameras, Valkyrie Black Eyes, Echo Yokai, Maestro Evil Eye, Zero ARGUS, Flores RCE-RATERO, Brava Kludge Drone, Iana Gemini, hacked observation tools
tips: Activate cloaking when drones are actively in your area to prevent intel gathering
Save cloaking for critical repositioning moments — early-round aggressive roaming
Don't cloak while shooting/sprinting — reveals position via glitch effect
Use 12-meter interference border as warning that drones are detecting you
Coordinate with roaming teammates — cloaking creates openings for flanks
Combine with other roamers for overlapping drone denial (Caveira, Kapkan, Lesion)

def.playstyle: Vigil is a fast, fragile roamer who denies attacker electronic intelligence through ERC-7 cloaking. His role is aggressive roaming while remaining invisible to drones, forcing attackers to coordinate without live drone feeds and rely on direct observation and callouts. His 3-speed/1-armor profile optimizes roaming and rapid repositioning. He combines cloaking for drone denial with pure gunfighting aggression. During early-round when drone activity is high, his ERC-7 provides maximum value. As round progresses, he transitions to pure roaming and gunfighting. Strategic cloaking usage around suspected drone positions prevents attackers from building map knowledge. He synergizes exceptionally well with other roamers (Caveira, Kapkan, Lesion) who create overlapping roaming pressure while Vigil denies electronic tracking. His success depends on aggressive positioning, gunplay skill, and timing ERC-7 activation to maximum value.
strengths: Complete removal from all attacker electronic surveillance (drones, cameras, hacked gadgets)
12-second max cloaking duration with unlimited recharge cycles per round
Infinite uses — unlimited activations/deactivations throughout round
Fast 3-speed allows rapid roaming and repositioning while cloaked
Doesn't glitch/reveal unless performing action (shooting, sprinting, gadget deployment)
Lion pings NOT triggered while cloaked (if activated before scan fires)
Thatcher EMP does NOT disable — only temporarily prevents activation
weaknesses: Fragile 1-armor profile — loses direct gunfights vs. equal-skill opponents
Glitches and reveals position if shoots, sprints, deploys gadgets, or melees while active
Still visible to direct attacker observation (only hides from electronic surveillance)
Footprints still visible to Jackal Eyenox
IQ Electronics Detector detects ERC-7 pack when activated
Thatcher/Impact EMP temporarily prevents activation (but doesn't destroy)
Twitch Shock Drone temporarily disables ERC-7

def.name: Warden

gadget.name: Glance Smart Glasses
gadget.description: Glance Smart Glasses — specialized eyewear that enables Warden to see through smoke and provides immunity to flash effects when activated. The glasses run on a rechargeable battery with a 20-second activation duration and a 9-second full recharge cycle. Activation requires manual trigger and is audible to nearby opponents.
mechanics: Manually activated via gadget button
Maximum 20 seconds of continuous active time per activation
Takes 9 seconds to completely recharge after use
Can only be reactivated once the recharge bar reaches 20% or higher
Infinite activations throughout the round (limited only by recharge time)
Complete immunity to blinding effects from:
Stun Grenades (all types)
Blitz's Flash Shield
tips: Activate glasses only when expecting incoming flashes or smoke pushes
Hold stationary positions in smoke for maximum visibility benefit
Movement degrades smoke vision significantly — stay still for best effect
Coordinate activation timing with team callouts to avoid wasting cooldown
Use flash immunity defensively after being flashed rather than preemptively
Remember that sound impairment from flashes persists even with glasses active

def.playstyle: Warden is a specialized 1-3 (Heavy) anchor designed to counter flash and smoke utility. He is a highly situational pick that becomes invaluable against compositions featuring Ying, Blitz, or heavy smoke breach strats, but provides minimal gadget value otherwise. His playstyle is reactive and position-focused — he holds anchored sites and denies attacker utility rather than roaming. Unlike roaming defenders, Warden's value comes from holding static positions where flash/smoke denial is most critical. His 1 speed means he commits fully to anchor play, making him a poor roamer. His heavy armor and access to Nitro Cell make him a durable site holder. With either the M590A1 for close-quarters dominance or MPX for sustained angle-holding, Warden can punish attackers who push into his pre-smoked or flash-denial position.
strengths: Extreme counter to Ying, Blitz, and all flash-heavy attacks
Allows holding positions completely untenable for other defenders against smoke/flash
Full immunity to flashes makes him invaluable against coordinated flash plays
Heavy 3-armor profile increases survivability when anchored
Can single-handedly deny smoke corridor pushes by holding visual control
Multiple activations available throughout round enables sustained utility denial
Observation Blockers add passive site denial
weaknesses: Highly situational — minimal gadget value against flash-light attack compositions
1 Speed makes him immobile — cannot reposition quickly once glass activation expires
Thatcher's EMP disables glasses for 10 seconds, completely negating gadget
IQ can detect active glasses with Electronics Detector
Activation is audible — attackers know he is active and can wait out timer
Smoke vision requires staying still — any movement degrades vision significantly
Glaz's Flip Sight can bypass Warden's smoke vision entirely (incomplete counter)

def.name: Aruni

gadget.name: Surya Laser Gate + Punch
gadget.description: **Gadget Setup:** Aruni has two distinct gadget capabilities: **Surya Laser Gate** (multi-use deployable) and an **augmented melee punch** (infinite use with per-wall limitation).

def.playstyle: Aruni is a heavy anchor (3/1 profile) designed to deny gadget-based attacks while providing flexible reinforcement through her punch tool. She functions as a hybrid defender—simultaneously denying breacher utility with Surya Gates while using her punch mechanic to create unexpected defensive positions and counter-reinforcements. Her Surya Gates serve as passive gadget destruction tools, destroying any projectile or drone that passes through. Unlike Jäger or Wamai, gates don't trigger passively—they require attacker gadgets to pass through. This forces attackers to expend utility to clear gate coverage, making her extremely valuable against drone-heavy or breacher-dependent attacks. Her punch tool provides flexibility unavailable to any other defender. Creating firing holes without full wall destruction allows her to hold unexpected angles, rotate through walls, and adapt site setup in real-time. Against Castle-heavy defensive setups, she's a direct counter—one punch destroys any Armor Panel. Mk 14 EBR provides precision fire at range for holding anchor positions, while P10 RONI offers aggressive close-quarters alternative. Barbed Wire adds area denial; Bulletproof Camera provides intel from reinforced positions.
strengths: Comprehensive gadget denial—gates destroy all projectiles and drones
Punch tool creates flexible reinforcement and unexpected defensive angles
Denies breacher-heavy attacks (Thermite, Hibana, Sledge breaching charges)
Durable (3 armor) allows holding aggressive anchor positions
Direct hard counter to Castle (punches destroy Armor Panels)
Indestructible gates persist entire round
Manual reactivation allows tactical timing control
weaknesses: Slow (1 speed) limits repositioning and round mobility
Punch mechanics limited to one effective punch per wall—not infinite flexibility
Gates do NOT damage attackers directly, only gadgets—pure utility denial
Punched holes can be exploited by attackers for counter-peeking and aggressive rotates
Vulnerable if caught away from gate coverage in open
Gate deactivation timers (30 seconds) provide windows for attacker pushes
Dependent on attacker gadget usage for primary value

def.name: Echo

gadget.name: Yokai Drones (×2)
gadget.description: Echo deploys two hovering drones called Yokai. Each drone can transition between ground mode (mobile, cannot fire bursts) and ceiling mode (immobile, can fire ultrasonic bursts). Only Echo can actively control the drones, but all defenders can view the drone camera feeds.
mechanics: Echo throws a Yokai drone to deploy; it starts in ground mode
On the ground, Yokai can move in all four directions but cannot use ultrasonic bursts
The drone's camera can pan up to 40 degrees upward but not downward
Yokai can jump vertically up to 10 meters; it has a 2-second cooldown between jumps
If a jump contacts a flat ceiling surface, the drone latches and enters ceiling mode
Once attached to the ceiling, Yokai becomes immobile
The drone's camera can pan downward freely but cannot pan upward
Echo can manually detach the drone from the ceiling to return to ground mode

def.playstyle: Echo is a stationary intel operator and anti-plant anchor. His primary strength is denying attacker objectives through persistent drone-based surveillance and active disruption via ultrasonic bursts. Echo excels at holding objective sites where he can position Yokai drones with maximum visibility of plant locations, particularly on maps with favorable ceiling geometry (bank, consulate). Echo's playstyle follows two distinct phases: (1) Early-Round: Yokai drones gather intelligence on attacker positioning and approach routes while remaining stationary. (2) Plant Phase: Echo pilots drones to disrupt and disorient attackers attempting the plant, either denying the plant outright or forcing repositioning. His 3-armor allows him to anchor site without dying to poke damage, keeping drone control active throughout.
strengths: Persistent Intel: Yokai drones provide real-time information to all defenders via shared camera feeds
Active Plant Denial: Ultrasonic bursts directly disrupt plant attempts and cancel continuous actions (defuser plant, charge deployment, etc.)
Ceiling Safety: Mounted drones are harder to destroy than ground-placed gadgets—attackers must locate and precisely shoot them
Flexible Control: Can pilot drones actively or leave them stationary; adapts to round phases
Heavy Armor: 3-armor allows Echo to anchor site and maintain active drone control under sustained pressure
Disorientation Advantage: Burst disorientation gives teammates a significant window to capitalize on confused attackers
weaknesses: Drone Destruction: Focused attacker fire can destroy drones; once destroyed, intel and disruption are denied
Limited Burst Charges: Finite charges per drone mean Echo must choose timing carefully; can be baited into wasting bursts
Positioning Dependent: Effectiveness heavily dependent on ceiling height and site geometry; some sites have poor drone placement
