Vulnerable While Piloting: Echo is distracted while remotely controlling drones; team must cover his position
IQ Hard Counter: IQ's Electronics Detector reveals Yokai positions and Echo's PDA location instantly, making drones vulnerable
Thatcher Hard Counter: EMP grenades disable drones for 10 seconds and force ceiling-attached drones to drop
Dokkaebi Hack: Logic Bomb hacks Yokai drones and removes Echo's control

def.name: Goyo

profile.role: Area Denial, Crowd Control, Explosive Defense
profile.seasonAdded: Y4S2 — Operation Ember Rise (Reworked Y7S1 — Operation Demon Veil)

gadget.name: Volcán Canisters (×4)
gadget.description: Goyo deploys four incendiary explosive canisters that can be placed on any surface (walls, floors, hatches, furniture, barricades). Each canister detonates when its red explosive section is struck by gunfire, explosives, or melee, releasing a large carpet of flames for area denial. The explosion is non-lethal to gadgets but creates significant environmental destruction and area denial.
mechanics: Goyo throws canisters to deploy them; placement takes approximately 4 seconds
Canisters can be placed on:
Floors (corridors, rooms)
Walls (vertical placement)
Hatches and hatch covers
Furniture and various environmental objects
Barricades (window or door)
Cannot be placed on Deployable Shields

def.playstyle: Goyo is a balanced area denial operator who specializes in controlling map space through incendiary barriers and explosive canisters. His playstyle revolves around strategic canister placement to deny high-traffic corridors, block key entryways, and force attackers into difficult positioning decisions. Unlike pure anchor operators, Goyo functions as a hybrid defender—capable of holding site, roaming to place canisters, or holding flexible defensive positions. Goyo's strategy unfolds in phases: (1) Setup Phase: Place 4 canisters on key entry points, narrow corridors, and plant site entry routes. (2) Early Round: Monitor canister positions via audio cues and teammate calls; use Proximity Alarms to track aggressive attacker pushes. (3) Mid-Round: Manage canister detonations—allow attackers to trigger them passively or coordinate manual detonations when attackers cluster. His balanced 2-speed/2-armor enables engagement in firefights or site holds.
strengths: Multi-Phase Area Denial: Four canisters create extensive denial zones across the map
Flame Spread Mechanics: Fire spreads through walls and hatches, denying area in multiple rooms from single canister
Chain Detonation: Proximity-placed canisters can create cascading explosions for larger denial zones
Low-Visibility Placement: Canisters placed below window sill are nearly invisible to vaulting attackers
Mechanical Advantage: Fire does NOT damage gadgets; canisters can be placed near utility without danger
Non-Lethal Damage: 36 DPS flame damage forces attackers to avoid crossing, creating psychological pressure
Flexibility: Balanced stats enable both site hold and flexible roaming with canister placement
weaknesses: Limited Charge Count: Only 4 canisters restrict coverage on very large maps
Requires Line of Sight to Trigger: Attackers behind cover cannot trigger canister from distance
Skilled Attackers Can Avoid: Experienced teams can identify canister positions and avoid triggering them
Hard Counters Available:
Ash's Breaching Rounds trigger explosions
Sledge's Hammer destroys canisters in one hit
Zofia's Impact Grenades trigger explosions

def.name: Mira

gadget.name: Black Mirror
gadget.description: Two Black Mirror one-way mirror installations — deployable onto reinforced walls only. Mirrors allow defenders to see clearly through the wall while blocking attacker vision from the opposite side (except through specific angles/extremes). Provides persistent intel throughout the round and can only be destroyed by attacking the vent panel at the base.
mechanics: 2 Black Mirrors per round
MUST be placed on reinforced walls only; unbreakable walls also acceptable
Placement requires reinforcement to be complete; cannot place on soft walls
Defender side: clear, unrestricted vision through the mirror
Attacker side: blocked vision; cannot see through (except from extreme angles)
Mirror persists until destroyed; provides intel for entire round
Provides sound passthrough: defenders hear attacker footsteps approaching
Large field of view (similar range to Black Eye cameras)
tips: Establish mirror placements early; setup-phase priority
Reinforce wall, then immediately place mirror before attackers arrive
Call out attacker positions seen through mirror to coordinate team response
Defend mirror positions to prevent vent destruction
Use mirror intel to time roamer rotations and deny attacker intel advantage
If mirror is destroyed, call it immediately; team loses intel source

def.playstyle: Mira is a heavy anchor specializing in one-way vision control and persistent intelligence gathering. Her playstyle centers on strategic wall reinforcement and mirror placement during setup phase, followed by anchoring near mirrors to defend them and act on gathered intel. She provides unmatched vision advantage in defended areas, allowing her team to prepare for incoming attacks. Mira's role is purely anchored; she is not a roamer and must stay site-proximal to protect mirrors and capitalize on intel. Her 3-armor and SMG/shotgun loadout support defensive anchor play. Success depends on wall choice, mirror placement, and coordination with team around intel callouts. She excels on objective-heavy sites with defined wall reinforcements and clear attacker approach routes. She struggles on open, roaming-centric sites or when hard-breach utilities threaten mirror positions early.
strengths: Unique One-Way Vision: Only operator providing unidirectional vision; attackers cannot counter-see
Persistent Intel: Mirrors provide information throughout round until destroyed; team-wide access
Defensive Advantage: Mirror positions protect defenders; sightline control at key chokes
Wall Reinforcement: Contributes to wall defense strategy beyond mirror placement
3-Armor Durability: Tank-like profile allows defending mirror positions under fire
Sound Passthrough: Hear attacker footsteps through mirror for additional intel
weaknesses: Wall Dependent: Mirrors ONLY placeable on reinforced walls; limited flexibility
Vent Vulnerability: Vent panel destruction removes mirror; easy to counter if exposed
Slow Movement: 1-speed limits repositioning; vulnerable if caught away from anchor position
Placement Vulnerability: Mira defenseless while placing mirror; can be caught during setup
Hard-Breach Threat: Thermite, Hibana, Ace, and wall destruction gadgets destroy mirrors directly
Static Position: Mirror intel comes from one location; cannot track mobile targets
IQ Detector: IQ electronics scanner reveals mirror presence through walls

def.name: Sentry

profile.ctu: Rainbow ROS / Keres Legion (double agent in lore)

gadget.name: Gadget Kit
gadget.description: Gadget Kit — Sentry selects any 2 types of defender secondary gadgets to bring into the field. One occupies the unique gadget slot. This gives Sentry unmatched flexibility to fill whatever gap the team's defensive setup is missing.
mechanics: Sentry carries two different secondary gadgets simultaneously — something no specialist can do
Effectively replaces two different specialist utility roles in a single operator slot
Useful combos:
Nitro Cell + Proximity Alarm — plant C4 underneath bomb, listen for alarm audio cue, detonate from safety
Observation Blocker + Bulletproof Camera — deny drone intel without exposing camera position
Deployable Shield + Nitro Cell / Impact Grenade — hold specific doorways or stairwells
tips: Use Sentry to fill whichever utility gap your lineup is missing — no Nitro Cell? Sentry brings one. Need a cam? Sentry covers it
Barbed Wire + Proximity Alarm is a strong passive combo for holding flanks without constant attention
On sites where you're short a camera or need to deny vertical play, Bulletproof Camera + Impact Grenades is a solid pick
Sentry's primary weapons are solid — don't treat him as a utility bot, he can frag too

primaries.notes: Versatile AR, good for most engagements
Semi-auto slug shotgun, strong at mid-range

secondaryGadgets: **Any 2 from:** Impact Grenade, Deployable Shield, Barbed Wire, Bulletproof Camera, Nitro Cell, Proximity Alarm, Observation Blocker

def.playstyle: Sentry is a utility-flexible anchor/support. Unlike specialist defenders with fixed gadgets, Sentry adapts to whatever the team needs. He plays best as a reactive pick — select the two gadgets that cover the weaknesses in your current lineup and play the utility-first role. He has no unique gadget gimmick to master, which also means no unique counter to play around. Straightforward to pick up and effective when used smartly to patch gaps.
strengths: Brings any two utility items — the most flexible secondary gadget kit in the game
Effectively doubles utility coverage for one operator slot
Strong primary weapon selection (TCSG12 is particularly potent)
No learning curve for the gadget — just bring what the team needs
No unique counter — attackers can't build specifically against Sentry
weaknesses: No unique gadget means no game-changing ability (no Smoke canisters, no Mira windows, no Jäger ADS)
Relies on team composition to determine his own value — weaker in a stacked/full-utility lineup
Cannot self-protect as well as specialists with active gadgets (Mira, Echo, Smoke)
In pro-level play, specialists almost always outvalue Sentry since utility efficiency is maximized elsewhere

def.name: Smoke

gadget.name: Remote Gas Canister
gadget.description: Three remote-detonated toxic gas canisters — deployable utility canisters filled with lethal gas (CSN - Calcitonin-Sensitive Neurotoxin). Canisters can be placed strategically around objective site; Smoke manually detonates them to fill areas with toxic gas denying plant/defuse attempts. Gas kills unprotected operators in 3-4 seconds; causes rapid health depletion.
mechanics: 3 gas canisters per round
Deployable on any solid surface (walls, floors, desks, shelves, etc.)
Placement requires line of sight; Smoke must see where canister lands
Canisters are invisible to attackers until detonated
No detonation timer; Smoke controls exactly when gas deploys
Canisters remain deployed throughout round until destroyed or detonated
Manually triggered by Smoke; requires activation during attack phase
Gas cloud expands rapidly and fills designated area within seconds
tips: Place canisters around objective site covering likely plant locations
Position canisters where they protect site from multiple angles (redundant coverage)
Hold canisters for post-plant phase; don't deploy early (gives attackers time to counter)
Coordinate gas timing with team: canisters should trigger when attackers are committed to plant/defuse
Use gas to deny plant attempts by controlling plant location with gas
Late-round canisters protect defuser; force attackers to enter gassed zone to defuse

def.playstyle: Smoke is a balanced-stat anchor specializing in post-plant denial and area control through toxic gas deployment. His core function is denying plant/defuse attempts through strategic gas placement and timing. Unlike roamers or pure fraggers, Smoke's role centers on site control and forcing attackers into gassed zones. Smoke's playstyle involves three phases: (1) Setup—place canisters around objective site covering plant locations, (2) Pre-plant—defend site with team, (3) Post-plant—manage gas timing to deny defuse attempts. His 2-2 stats allow flexible anchor or secondary roaming roles. Success depends on canister placement accuracy, managing detonation timing, and coordinating with team around gas denial. Smoke excels on objective sites with clear plant locations and defensible geometry. He is weaker on wide-open sites where gas has limited impact. Best used with site-anchoring team compositions that value controlled deny strategies.
strengths: Post-Plant Denial: Most powerful defuse-denial tool in the game; forces attackers into gassed zones
Area Control: Gas denies specific objective areas; forces attacker repositioning
Flexible Timing: Manual detonation allows Smoke to trigger gas exactly when needed
Multiple Canisters: Three gas canisters allow redundant coverage of plant locations
Invisible Gadget: Canisters undetectable until detonated; maintains surprise element
Balanced Stats: 2-2 profile allows flexible anchor or secondary roaming roles
Synergy Value: Works with Maestro intel (identifies plant locations) for coordinated denial
weaknesses: Destructible Canisters: Attackers can destroy canisters with utility before detonation
Limited Coverage: 3 canisters insufficient to cover all plant locations on large sites
Timing Skill: Optimal gas deployment requires game sense and timing accuracy
Vulnerable While Detonating: Smoke must be alive and present to detonate gas
Attacker Immunity: Protected operators (Doc, certain events) unaffected by gas
IQ Counter: IQ's Electronics Detector reveals canister locations through walls
Thermite/Hibana/Ace Counter: Hard-breacher utility can destroy canisters before detonation

def.name: Thorn

gadget.name: Razorbloom Shell
gadget.description: Razorbloom Shell — a proximity grenade that can be thrown and attached to any surface. When an attacker enters its trigger radius, the device activates and propels sharp blades in all directions after a brief delay, delivering lethal damage to all nearby targets.
mechanics: Can be thrown and attached to any surface (walls, floors, ceilings, gadgets)
Proximity-activated: triggers when attacker enters radius
Upon triggering, activates a short timer before blade deployment
Distinctive triggering noise alerts Thorn and teammates
Brief detonation delay gives attackers time to react/escape
Explosion deals significant lethal damage in all directions
Can be destroyed by distant explosions, gunfire, or laser devices (Twitch Shock Drone, Zero's ARGUS)
IQ can spot Razorblooms with Electronics Scanner
tips: Place grenades in high-traffic chokepoints and doorways
Use around planted bomb to deny defuse attempts
Stack multiple grenades to create comprehensive denial zones
Place where attackers cannot easily destroy or avoid them
Coordinate with team to exploit grenades — attackers cluster when avoiding them
Combine with gadgets that slow attackers (Barbed Wire, Melusi Banshee, Clash Shield, Lesion Gu Mines) to prevent escape

def.playstyle: Thorn is a balanced anchor who combines area denial through explosive grenades with flexible site defense. Her playstyle revolves around strategic grenade placement to control key areas and deny attacker advances, particularly in post-plant scenarios. She excels at forcing attackers into difficult decisions — either risk the explosion, spend time/utility destroying grenades, or reposition entirely. Her 2-2 stats allow her to hold angles effectively while managing her grenade placement. The warning noise and detonation delay create tactical tension that benefits defenders. She plays best near site where grenades provide maximum defensive value. The warning audio serves as early alert for her team, allowing coordinated responses to grenade triggers. Combining her grenades with gadgets that slow attackers (Barbed Wire, Melusi) maximizes area denial effectiveness.
strengths: Exceptional post-plant area denial through proximity grenades
Forces attacker decision-making (risk explosion, destroy grenade, or reposition)
Warning noise alerts defenders to attacker pushes
Can be placed on most surfaces for flexible positioning
Detonation deals significant lethal damage in large radius
Multiple grenades create overlapping denial zones
Balanced 2-2 stats enable both holding angles and managing grenades
weaknesses: Twitch, Zero's laser devices can destroy grenades from range
IQ can detect grenades with Electronics Scanner
Thatcher EMP and Impact EMP disable grenades
Brava Kludge Drone can hack grenades to target defenders
Warning timer gives skilled attackers time to react/escape
Grenades require proper placement for maximum value
Limited to single Deployable Shield in secondary gadgets

def.name: Maestro

gadget.name: Compact Laser Emplacement (Evil Eye)
gadget.description: Three Compact Laser Emplacements (CLE-V), colloquially known as Evil Eyes — bulletproof, gyroscopic, remotely-operated turrets equipped with high-energy laser and swivel-mounted camera. Maestro deploys up to 3 Evil Eyes on any solid surface (walls, floors, ceilings). Each camera can see through smoke, provides persistent intel, and fires damaging laser bursts when activated.
mechanics: 3 Evil Eyes per round; deployable on walls, floors, or any solid surface
Cannot be deployed on reinforced walls (gadget is connected to surface; wall destruction = gadget destruction)
Best placed on unbreakable surfaces or walls that won't be destroyed
All teammates can view Evil Eye cameras; only Maestro can fire the laser
Large range of motion similar to Valkyrie's Black Eye
Wall-mounted: 180° swivel maximum
Floor-mounted: full 360° swivel
Can see through smoke (vision unaffected by smoke grenades)
tips: Place Evil Eyes on objective site with clear sightlines to plant locations and attacker approach routes
Unbreakable walls (exterior, reinforced concrete) are safest placement locations
Use evil eyes as persistent intel sources: toggle between open (intel/firing) and closed (protection) based on attacker proximity
Laser is most effective at pressuring plant attempts, forcing attackers to relocate or suppress you
Coordinate laser firing with teammates to avoid friendly fire and maximize damage output
Manage heat cycles: 24-shot limit means strategic bursts (e.g., 12 shots + 3-second pause + 12 shots avoids full overheat)

primaries.notes: Primary weapon; high ammo capacity for sustained fire

def.playstyle: Maestro is a heavy anchor specializing in persistent intel and active area denial through Evil Eye laser pressure. Unlike pure intel operators, Maestro's Evil Eyes provide guaranteed camera feeds even when threatened, and the laser turret allows active pressure on attackers. His 3-armor profile allows him to absorb significant damage while holding site; his ALDA LMG sustains fire for extended engagements. Maestro's playstyle centers on Evil Eye placement during setup phase, followed by anchoring near cameras or on-site. He monitors feeds, fires lasers at plant attempts or push engagements, and relies on his team to protect camera positions. His effectiveness depends on camera placement sightlines, laser timing discipline, and team communication around intel callouts. Maestro excels on objective-centric sites with good camera placement (Bank, Consulate, Coastline). He struggles on maps with limited placement opportunities or roaming-heavy defensive structures. Success requires balancing intel gathering with offensive laser pressure while managing heat cycles.
strengths: Persistent Bulletproof Intel: Three Evil Eyes provide overlapping coverage and redundant intel sources throughout the round
Laser Area Denial: Active laser pressure forces attackers to suppress you, relocate, or take damage during critical moments
Survives Surface Destruction Partially: Cameras placed on reinforced walls are destroyed with wall, but those on unbreakable surfaces persist
Smoke Penetration: Evil Eyes see through smoke, providing info when attackers smoke site
Heavy Armor: 3-armor allows tank-like hold on site under fire; ALDA LMG provides sustained suppressive firepower
Versatile Gadget Options: Barbed Wire, Impact Grenades, or Observation Blocker adapts to site needs
weaknesses: Slow Movement: 1-speed profile makes repositioning difficult; once engaged, hard to rotate
EMP Hard Counter: Thatcher's EMP forces Evil Eyes open, rendering them instantly destroyable; denies Maestro's primary tool
Laser Emitter Vulnerability: When firing, laser emitter is exposed; skilled attackers can destroy eyes while Maestro aims
Limited Destruction Resilience: Evil Eyes destroyed = permanent intel loss for that camera; no way to redeploy
Dokkaebi Hack: Logic Bomb allows attackers to view Evil Eye feeds, losing tactical advantage
Heat Management: 24-shot limit before 5-second overheat demands disciplined fire; cannot sustain continuous pressure
Destructible by Multiple Tools: IQ scanner detects all eyes; grenades, Sledge, Maverick, Kali, Flores, Zero all have direct counters

def.name: Mozzie

gadget.name: Pest Drones
gadget.description: Three autonomous Pest spider drones — small, autonomous AI-controlled drones that automatically hunt and capture attacker drones within detection radius. Each Pest is a roving defense platform that converts enemy reconnaissance into friendly assets.
mechanics: 3 Pest drones per round; autonomous once deployed
Pests patrol/stay in deployment area automatically
Require no manual control once placed; operate autonomously
Remain active for entire round unless destroyed
Pests automatically detect attacker drones in proximity (must have line of sight)
When attacker drone detected, Pest races toward target drone
Pest captures attacker drone, integrating it into Mozzie's drone fleet
Mozzie gains full control of captured drone
tips: Deploy Pests early in predicted drone pathways and entry corridors
Place Pests in high-traffic areas where attackers send drones frequently
Use captured drones for post-plant intel gathering and positioning
Manage captured drone count (can control multiple simultaneously)
Call out captured drone locations to team
Use Pests defensively; most effective when unseen

def.playstyle: Mozzie is a light roamer specializing in drone denial and information advantage. His core value is converting attacker reconnaissance into friendly assets—a net-positive information trade. Rather than destroying drones, Mozzie's Pests capture and flip them, creating dual-layer benefit: denying attacker intel while gaining new intel sources. Mozzie's playstyle centers on early Pest deployment in predicted drone lanes, followed by aggressive roaming with captured drone intel. His 3-speed enables aggressive repositioning and drone-guided rotations. He operates best with high map knowledge and communication with roaming teammates. Unlike pure anchors, Mozzie is mobile and can leverage captured drone intelligence to catch attackers off-guard. His playstyle flexes based on team composition: pure roamer with other anchors, secondary anchor with roaming-light defense, or intel gatherer supporting team rotations. Success depends on effective Pest placement, managing captured drones, and communication with team.
strengths: Information Net Gain: Captures enemy drones instead of destroying them; denies + gains intel simultaneously
Autonomous Operation: Pests work without manual control; Mozzie plays freely while Pests hunt
High Speed Roaming: 3-speed enables aggressive repositioning and rotation with drone intel
Flexible Gadget: Works against any attacker drone type; value against drone-heavy attacks
Multiple Information Streams: Can control multiple captured drones simultaneously
Denial + Utility: Pests deter attackers from sending drones; can clear drones from site
Excellent Counter: Direct counter to Twitch, Valkyrie, IQ, and attacker reconnaissance focus
weaknesses: Light Armor: 1-armor makes Mozzie extremely fragile in direct firefights
Gadget Dependent: No value if attackers avoid drone deployment
Pest Fragility: Small targets can be destroyed with focused fire
EMP Vulnerability: Thatcher disables all Pests for 10 seconds (reset on timer end)
Passive Defense: Cannot actively destroy drones; only captures them passively
Requires Drone Presence: Relies on attackers actually sending drones early/frequently
Destroyed Drone Loss: Captured drones can be destroyed; permanently lost if destroyed

def.name: Pulse

gadget.name: Heartbeat Sensor
gadget.description: Handheld heartbeat detection scanner — passive intel device detecting living attackers through walls and obstacles within detection radius. Displays real-time attacker positions, direction, and distance without alerting targets.
mechanics: Detects all living attackers within approximately 7-9 meter radius
Works through walls, reinforced walls, floors, hatches, and all obstacles
Passive detection; does not alert attackers to Pulse's presence
Displays compass-style direction indicator showing attacker bearing
Shows distance estimation and number of detected heartbeats
No cooldown; can be used continuously throughout the round
No resource drain; operates infinitely once activated
Requires line of sight to walls/obstacles between Pulse and attacker (no X-ray through complex geometry)
tips: During setup phase, use sensor to identify attacker entry and approach routes
Position yourself on predicted attack paths before attackers arrive; sensor confirms approach timing
Call out attacker counts, positions, and directions to roaming teammates
Rotate between multiple roaming positions; don't stay predictable in one location
Use sensor timing to coordinate multi-angle attacks with nearby roamers
On vertical maps, use sensor to track attackers approaching stairs/hatches for catches

def.playstyle: Pulse is a light roamer specializing in real-time intel gathering through his heartbeat sensor. Unlike passive intel operators (Valkyrie, Maestro), Pulse provides active, live attacker position data that he can leverage for aggressive ambush plays. His core role is hunt-based roaming: position based on sensor intel, ambush attackers from predicted routes, retreat/rotate when threatened. Pulse's playstyle centers on three behaviors: (1) Early-round scanning to identify attacker approach vectors, (2) Proactive positioning on likely attacker routes with coordinated roaming teammates, (3) Continuous rotation to avoid predictability. His 3-speed enables rapid repositioning; his 1-armor makes direct duels unfavorable. Success depends on map knowledge, sensor reading accuracy, team coordination, and aggressive but intelligent positioning. He excels on vertical maps (Villa, Bank, Coastline) with multiple floor-to-floor roaming paths. He struggles on open, flat layouts where roaming becomes predictable. Best used with communication-heavy teams that can coordinate ambushes around sensor callouts.
strengths: Real-Time Intel: Live attacker positioning without alerting them
Passive Gadget: Sensor provides continuous intel with no resource drain
Roaming Enabler: Intel allows proactive ambush positioning rather than defensive waiting
High Speed: 3-speed enables rapid repositioning and escape
Flexible Roaming: Works on vertical and horizontal maps with good adaptation
Team Coordination: Excellent intel callout tool for coordinating team ambushes
Early-Round Value: Sensor identifies attacker entry and breach locations immediately
weaknesses: Light Armor: 1-armor makes Pulse extremely fragile in direct firefights
Range Limitation: 7-9 meter sensor range is medium-distance; doesn't work far away
Team Dependent: Requires teammates positioned nearby to capitalize on intel callouts
Predictable Roaming: Without good team coordination, Pulse becomes predictable
Montagne Counter: Montagne's shield blocks sensor line of sight; blocks intel gathering
IQ Counter: IQ's Electronics Detector reveals Pulse through walls
Caveira Counter: Caveira's interrogation forces Pulse info extraction

def.name: Solis

gadget.name: SPEC-IO Electro-Sensor
gadget.description: SPEC-IO Electro-Sensor — an augmented reality headset with integrated bracelet and gloves that detects and displays all active electronic gadgets deployed by attackers. Provides real-time electronic signature detection with overlay display and team-coordinated scanning capabilities.
mechanics: Detects all electronic gadgets within range (drones, breach charges, phones, etc.)
Standard mode: displays electronic signatures as hexagonal tile outlines
Does NOT identify gadget type until Overdrive activation
Detection range: exactly 15 meters
Cannot detect defuser while being carried (only when dropped or planted)
Cannot be used during Preparation Phase
Activates 3 times per round
Activating Overdrive identifies what type of gadget is present
tips: Use Overdrive strategically to identify specific gadget types on key detections
Place Cluster Scan before major attacker pushes for team-wide gadget intel
Use Impact Grenades with scanner active to counter detected gadgets through walls
Scan early round to identify attacker drone patterns
Identify Claymores/Airjabs outside windows for teammates to avoid or destroy
Coordinate scans with team to provide continuous gadget information

def.playstyle: Solis is an intel-focused operator who provides comprehensive electronic gadget detection without directly destroying attacker utility. She excels at information gathering and team coordination through gadget detection and identification. Her balanced 2-2 stats allow her to hold angles while maintaining scanner capability. Her playstyle revolves around frequent scanning and strategic Overdrive activation to maximize intel value. Unlike gadget-denial operators, she enables teammates to adapt positioning based on known gadget deployment. The SPEC-IO is most valuable early-round for identifying attacker strategies and drone patterns. She plays best as a flexible defender who rotates strategically while maintaining scanner coverage of key areas. Her ability to destroy gadgets through walls with Impact Grenades while scanning adds offensive capability. She provides value throughout the round by identifying utility deployment and alerting teammates to threats.
strengths: Detects all electronic gadgets through walls and obstacles
Cluster Scans provide team-wide gadget intelligence
Early-round gadget identification reveals attacker strategy
Can destroy detected gadgets with Impact Grenades through walls
Balanced 2-2 stats enable both combat and intel gathering
Overdrive identification exposes specific gadget types
Provides continuous value throughout entire round
weaknesses: IQ's Electronics Detector can detect and ping active SPEC-IO
Thatcher's EMP and Impact EMP disable the SPEC-IO temporarily
Does not prevent gadget deployment — only provides intelligence
Temporarily unarmed while using scanner (vulnerable to rushes)
Overdrive activation alerts attackers to her presence
Cannot identify gadgets without Overdrive activation
Attackers without electronics (melee rushes) bypass her utility entirely

def.name: Valkyrie

gadget.name: Gyro Cam Mk2 "Black Eye"
gadget.description: Black Eye Gyro Cam Mk2 ×3 — throwable sticky cameras with gyroscopic lenses that attach to surfaces and provide color-tinted surveillance feeds. Cameras rotate 180+ degrees horizontally and vertically with fast rotation speeds and can be accessed by all defending teammates.
mechanics: Deploy up to 3 Black Eye cameras per round
Throw cameras and they stick to solid surfaces (walls, ceilings, floors, outdoor surfaces)
Cameras encased in silicone ball with micro-suction cups
Internal gyroscope stabilizes lens angle
Takes time to fully attach before feed becomes available
Can access camera feeds while in mid-air, but video doesn't begin until attached to surface
Gyroscopic lens allows 180+ degree horizontal/vertical rotation
Faster rotation speed than standard CCTV cameras
tips: Place first Black Eye outside during setup phase for pre-attack attacker positioning intel
Position second Black Eye on objective site for plant-denial information
Position third Black Eye on secondary roam location or backup plant position
Maximize coverage by placing cameras in high angles, hidden spots, hard-to-reach areas
Communicate camera locations and active feeds to team
Mark destroyed camera locations for team callouts and positioning

def.playstyle: Valkyrie is a balanced intel operator who provides team-wide camera surveillance throughout the round. Her role is strategic camera placement during setup, followed by flexible positioning (roaming or site defense) while teammates utilize camera information for tactical advantage. Unlike pure roamers, Valkyrie's primary value is information sharing. Her 2-2 stats allow flexible positioning — she can roam, anchor, or support defensively based on round development. During setup, she deploys 3 cameras in high-value positions (outside for pre-attack intel, objective site for post-plant denial, roam site for ambush support). Her success depends on camera placement quality and team coordination. Well-placed cameras provide consistent intel throughout the round, even after her death. She pairs well with roamers who use camera information to identify and exploit isolated attackers, and with anchors who use camera intel to prepare for incoming assaults.
strengths: Persistent team intel throughout entire round (cameras active even after Valkyrie death)
Unique outside placement capability for pre-attack attacker positioning information
Small, sticky cameras difficult for attackers to locate and systematically destroy
Team-wide camera access — all defenders can pilot any Black Eye for maximum intel coverage
Balanced 2-2 stats allow flexible positioning (roaming, site defense, support)
Color-tinted feeds with wide FOV and fast rotation provide superior intel to standard CCTV
Can retrieve and redeploy cameras for flexible positioning adjustment
weaknesses: Thatcher EMP disables cameras for 10 seconds (doesn't destroy but denies access)
IQ Electronics Detector can detect and coordinate camera destruction
Twitch Shock Drone and Zero ARGUS lasers destroy cameras remotely
Dokkaebi can hack deceased defender phone and turn Black Eyes against defending team
Nøkk HEL Presence Reduction wipes her image from all surveillance tools
Static information source — cameras cannot follow moving attackers
Destroyed cameras permanently gone — loss of key placement impacts round

def.name: Fenrir

profile.role: Area Denial, Roam Disruption, Crowd Control

gadget.name: F-NATT Dread Mines (×4)
gadget.description: Fenrir deploys four F-NATT Dread Mines that can stick to any surface and emit a hallucinogenic fear-inducing gas. Fenrir carries 2 activation codes and can toggle mines on/off remotely from any distance. Once activated, mines detect nearby attackers and release purple gas that obscures visibility and disrupts hearing.
mechanics: Fenrir throws mines to deploy; they stick to walls, ceilings, and floors
Mines begin in a deactivated state (white HUD indicator)
Fenrir must manually activate mines using his 2 activation codes (1 second activation time, Fenrir becomes unarmed during activation)
While deactivated, mines are harmless and cannot be triggered
Fenrir's HUD displays all mine locations with status indicators (white = deactivated, cyan = activated, red = triggered)
Fenrir can pick up and redeploy deactivated mines
Fenrir can face a mine and activate/deactivate it remotely from any distance
Only 2 mines can be active at once (limited by activation codes)

def.playstyle: Fenrir is a balanced area denial operator designed to disrupt attacker positioning through fear-inducing gas and psychological pressure. Unlike passive trappers (Kapkan, Lesion), Fenrir actively controls his mines remotely, allowing dynamic activation timing based on real-time attacker positioning. His 2-speed/2-armor profile provides flexibility to engage attackers directly while managing mine control. Fenrir's playstyle centers on creating zones of denial and uncertainty. By strategically placing mines in high-traffic roaming corridors and toggling them based on attacker proximity, he forces attackers into cautious, predictable patterns. The fear gas induces disorientation and vision loss, creating openings for defending roamers to capitalize. Fenrir excels in coordinated team play where roamers can exploit mine-induced confusion for eliminations.
strengths: Remote Mine Control: Strategic activation timing allows adaptation to real-time attacker positioning (unlike passive traps)
Extensive Area Denial: Four mines create multiple denial zones across the map
Psychological Pressure: Fear gas induces disorientation without direct damage; forces cautious attacker behavior
Roam Disruption: Mines in corridors slow attacker advances and create opportunities for roaming defenders to catch distracted targets
Flexible Redeployment: Can pick up and redeploy deactivated mines based on attacker push locations
Balanced Stats: 2-speed/2-armor enables both mine control and direct fragging capability
Intel Advantage: Mine activation provides audio alerts confirming attacker presence and positioning
weaknesses: Limited Activation Codes: Only 2 mines can be active simultaneously; must manage limited resources
No Direct Damage: Fear gas disorientation does not eliminate attackers; requires teammate follow-up
Electronic Vulnerability: EMP (Thatcher, Impact EMP), IQ scanner, and Twitch drone all disable/destroy mines
Brava Hard Counter: Kludge Drone hacks activated mines, turning them against defenders
Sledge One-Shot: Sledge destroys any mine with one hit
Exposed During Activation: 1-second activation window leaves Fenrir unarmed and vulnerable
Limited Effective Range: Gas effects only apply to attackers in proximity radius

def.name: Frost

gadget.name: Welcome Mat / Sterling MK2 LHT (×3)
gadget.description: Frost deploys three mechanical leg-hold bear traps called "Welcome Mats." These spring-loaded traps can be placed on any floor surface (entrances, windows, hatches, corridor passages). When an attacker vaults or steps on a trap, they are instantly placed in a DBNO (Down But Not Out) state.
mechanics: Frost throws/places traps to deploy them on floors
Traps take 3 seconds to deploy
Frost's hitbox becomes vertically smaller while deploying (crouch-like profile), allowing deployment in tight spaces or under windows during combat
Traps can be placed on any floor surface: beneath windows, in doorways, on hatches, against walls, under tables
Tight spaces may prevent placement in rare circumstances
Defender gadgets like Alibi's Prisma or Lesion's Gu Mines can prevent Welcome Mat placement, but most other devices can be placed on top of mats
Trap triggers when ANY attacker steps on it or vaults over it (trap activates on contact)
Instant DBNO: Trapped attacker is immediately placed in a Down But Not Out state (completely incapacitated)

def.playstyle: Frost is a balanced roaming trapper who specializes in instant incapacitation through mechanical bear trap placement. Unlike explosive trappers (Kapkan) who damage/disrupt, Frost removes attackers from play entirely through DBNO state, creating guaranteed execution opportunities. Her 2-speed/2-armor profile provides flexibility to both trap management and direct combat. Frost's playstyle centers on trap placement during setup phase followed by active roaming. She places 3 traps on high-traffic entry points (windows prioritized for vault mechanics), then roams to adjacent defensive positions. When traps trigger, she either executes downed targets immediately or coordinates with nearby teammates for guaranteed elimination. Her success depends on trap placement intuition, teammate proximity, and quick reaction to trap activation audio cues. Frost excels on maps with extensive window systems (Chalet, Consulate, Kafe) where traps can be layered across multiple entry routes. She struggles on maps with few entry points or when teammates cannot position near traps for execution.
strengths: Instant Incapacitation: Downed attackers are completely removed from combat immediately; no follow-up damage needed
Guaranteed Execution: Downed state creates guaranteed kill opportunity; teammate revive is the only escape
Mechanical Immunity: Welcome Mats are immune to electronic countermeasures (EMP, IQ scanner, Twitch, etc.)
Invisible Threat: Well-placed traps are extremely difficult to spot; attackers often vault before noticing
DBNO Mechanics: One downed attacker per round means repeated trap triggers guarantee kills (second down = death)
Layerable Utility: 3 traps can be concentrated on critical entry points or spread across map
Window Specialization: Particularly effective against vault routes; forces slow approach or trap destruction
weaknesses: Limited Charges: Only 3 traps restrict coverage on large maps with many entry points
Requires Coordination: Trap value maximized when teammates are positioned to execute; isolated traps are less effective
Predictable Placement: Experienced attackers learn common trap spots and destroy proactively
Trap Visibility: Attackers can learn trap positions and avoid them with careful approach
Downed Not Killed: First trap only downs; revive by teammates can save trapped attacker (second down ensures death)
Vulnerability During Execution: Frost is stationary while executing downed target; exposed to teammate fire
Map Dependent: Effectiveness varies dramatically based on entry point density and geometry

def.name: Kapkan

gadget.name: EDD MK II Entry Denial Device (×5)
gadget.description: Kapkan deploys five laser tripwire mines on doorways and windows. Each mine creates an invisible horizontal laser beam across the entry point. When any attacker crosses the laser, the mine detonates for 60 damage within 3-meter radius. Mines must be placed on barricadable entry frames; they trigger on attacker contact only (friendlies cannot trigger, drones pass through).
mechanics: 5 EDD mines per round; attach to doorway/window frames
Invisible horizontal laser tripwire across entry
Multiple mines can stack on same frame
Placement height varies: windows chest-height minimum, head-height maximum; doorways ankle-to-head height
Laser triggers when ANY attacker touches it
60 damage per detonation; 3-meter effective blast radius (drops off harshly)
No friendly fire—defenders cannot trigger mines
Drones pass through unaffected

def.playstyle: Kapkan is a balanced roaming trapper who specializes in early-round damage through laser tripwire mines. Unlike passive trappers, Kapkan places mines for area denial, then actively roams to capitalize on weakened/distracted attackers. His 2-speed/2-armor enables both trap management and direct fragging. Kapkan's strategy: Place mines on forced entry routes during setup. Listen for detonations to locate attackers. Roam to secondary positions to finish weakened targets or ambush distracted units clearing mines.
strengths: Free Early Damage: Mines damage attackers immediately upon entry—soft damage before gunfights
5 Mine Coverage: More mines than comparable trappers; layerable on key entries
Roaming Flexibility: Balanced stats enable holding or roaming; not locked into anchor
Experienced Player Performance: Grant (stack player) achieves 2.07 K/D with Kapkan—proven elite-level effectiveness
weaknesses: Visible When Deployed: Mines visible unless perfectly hidden in environmental obstruction
Skill Dependent: Beginner placement is ineffective; experienced teams destroy mines instantly
Limited Damage: 60 damage doesn't guarantee kills; value is softening, not finishing
Drones Counter Mines: Attacker drones scout and mark mine positions for easy clearing

def.name: Lesion

gadget.name: Gu Mine (×8)
gadget.description: Lesion deploys eight invisible poison mines that are placed at floor level. When an attacker steps on a mine, it lodges in their body, dealing ~5 HP/second poison damage while revealing their health status to Lesion on a gadget display. Attackers can remove mines by spending ~5 seconds pulling them out (unable to move during removal). Multiple mines can lodge in same attacker, stacking damage and debuffs.
mechanics: 8 Gu mines per round; placed at floor level on any surface
Invisible to attackers; detection requires close inspection
Triggers when any attacker steps on it
Mine lodges in attacker's body upon contact
Damage Rate: ~5 HP/second (approx. 60 HP per 12-second engagement)
Duration: Persists until attacker removes mine or dies
Health Tracking: Lesion sees poisoned attacker's health status on gadget display; rough location revealed
Multiple Stacks: Multiple mines on same attacker stack damage and debuffs

def.playstyle: Lesion is a balanced roaming trapper specializing in attrition and information gathering. Unlike instant-incapacitation trappers (Frost) or burst-damage trappers (Kapkan), Lesion provides gradual poison damage and health intel. His 8 mines allow extensive coverage; his 2-speed/2-armor enables flexible roaming. Lesion places mines on roam paths during setup, then rotates to secondary positions. Poisoned attackers are weakened gradually while Lesion gains health information via gadget display. He calls out poisoned attacker positions/health to teammates, enabling coordinated engagement on weakened targets.
strengths: Persistent Attrition: Poison damage continues throughout engagement; weakens even without direct follow-up
Health Intelligence: Lesion sees poisoned attacker health; provides intel without drone/camera
8 Mine Capacity: Most mines of any trapper; enables extensive area coverage
Invisible & Sticky: Difficult to spot if well-placed; surprise damage advantage
Stacking Potential: Multiple mines create severe health drain and debuff
Roaming Flexibility: 2-speed enables roaming after placement; hunt weakened targets
weaknesses: Gradual Damage: Poison is slow; doesn't immediately threaten unlike burst trappers
Mine Removal: Attackers can remove mines; spending 5 seconds creates 5-second window for team engagement
Moderate Durability: 2-speed/2-armor is moderate; direct gunfight vulnerability if caught alone
Utility Vulnerability: Grenades and utilities destroy multiple mines if focused on mine locations
Delayed Value: Unlike Kapkan (instant) or Frost (incapacitation), Lesion's value is conditional and delayed

def.name: Melusi

gadget.name: Banshee Sonic Defence
gadget.description: Four Banshee Sonic Defence devices — deployable, bulletproof sound emitters generating standing waves (18-19 Hz) that produce disorienting effects and movement slowdown on nearby attackers. Each Banshee is stationary, autonomous (requires no manual control), and creates a dual-layer effect: audio alert + speed reduction.
mechanics: 4 Banshee devices per round; deployable on walls or floors
Devices are bulletproof when inactive; can only be destroyed by explosives or melee attacks
Gunfire does not damage closed Banshees
Banshees operate autonomously once placed—no user control required
Activates when attackers enter radius AND have line of sight to device
Activation emits loud buzzing noise alerting all defenders
Effect applies even to prone attackers
Applies even through drone holes and small soft wall destruction gaps
tips: Place Banshees in high-traffic corridors and common roam routes for early-round intel
Stack multiple Banshees in narrow choke points for maximum slowdown (effects stack)
Position Banshees at door/window entrances to slow aggressive pusher entry
Use audio alerts to call out attacker positions to team
Banshees act as radar—activation tells defenders attackers are pushing
Pair Banshees with traps (Frost mats, Kapkan traps) to catch slowed attackers

def.playstyle: Melusi is a heavy anchor specializing in area denial and crowd-control through sonic disruption. Unlike roamers or aggressive operators, she functions as a defensive anchor who uses passive Banshee placement to create denial zones. Her 3-armor and slow movement speed position her as a site-oriented defender rather than a mobile responder. Melusi's value centers on information gathering (audio alerts when Banshees activate) and forcing attackers into controlled entry paths by making roaming space hostile. Her Banshees are particularly effective against aggressive entry teams or coordinated pushes into anchored positions. The slow effect combined with teammate coordination can turn pushed attackers into vulnerable targets. She excels on sites with defined roaming corridors where Banshee placement can funnel attacker movement. On open, spread-out sites, her effectiveness diminishes. Success depends on anticipating attacker routes and placing Banshees in high-impact positions early.
strengths: Persistent Area Denial: Four Banshees create extensive denial zones without resource consumption
Dual Effect: Audio alert + movement slowdown provides both intel and active pressure
Stacking Slowdown: Multiple overlapping Banshees create near-total movement lockdown on narrow choke points
Autonomous Operation: Once placed, Banshees require no manual control—Melusi can play normally
Heavy Armor: 3-armor profile allows holding defensive positions under fire
Trap Synergy: Slowed attackers are vulnerable to Frost, Kapkan, tachanka traps
Information Tool: Banshee activation tells team attackers are pushing specific routes
weaknesses: Slow Movement Speed: 1-speed limits repositioning and rotation; vulnerable if caught away from site
Predictable Placement: Experienced attackers expect Banshee placement in corridors
Explosive Vulnerability: Attackers can destroy Banshees with grenades or utility
Finka Counter: Finka's Adrenal Surge bypasses slowdown (Banshee still alerts)
Brava Hack: Kludge Drone hacks Banshee, turning it against defenders
Does Not Stop Pushes: Slowdown deters but does not prevent determined attacker entry
Limited Active Contribution: Banshees are passive; Melusi provides limited direct combat pressure

def.name: Alibi

gadget.name: Prisma
gadget.description: Prisma — a pair of holographic decoys that project volumetric displays of Alibi. Each decoy is a three-dimensional illusion created using laser light suspended dust particles, displayed at over 1600 dpi resolution. When activated, a Prisma displays a static image of Alibi in default uniform holding an unmodified Mx4 Storm in standing position.
mechanics: Deploy up to 3 Prismas during the round
Prismas activate when thrown on the floor with sufficient space (minimum floor clearance required)
Activation takes 1 second — Prisma unfolds and projects volumetric display
If insufficient space, Prisma enters "jammed" state with blinking red light and fails to deploy
Prismas are destroyed if a barricade is placed on a doorway where they sit
Display shows Alibi in standing, idle position
Model faces the same direction as the thrower when deployed
Display is completely static (no animation or movement)

def.playstyle: Alibi is a high-speed roamer designed to gather intelligence and disrupt attacker confidence through deception. Her Prisma decoys work best when placed early in the round to establish baseline intel on attacker approaches and rotations. She is NOT an anchor—she roams the map using her decoys as intelligence tools to ping inquisitive attackers and coordinate team responses. The Prisma gadget functions as both a psychological deterrent and an information-gathering tool. Attackers hesitate to push past decoys for fear of revealing their position, and Alibi can capitalize on this hesitation to catch rotators off-guard. Her three-speed rating makes her a pure roamer; she excels at information gathering and disruption but lacks the durability for extended firefights. Mx4 Storm provides solid DPS for roaming engagements. ACS12 shotgun offers alternative close-quarters pressure. Observation Blockers allow her to deny line of sight while gathering information.
strengths: Strong information gathering through decoy pings visible to all defenders
High speed (3/1 profile) enables aggressive roaming and map pressure
Decoys create psychological pressure and waste attacker time and utility
Low-risk intel tool—decoys cost nothing if not triggered
Excellent at identifying attacker positions early; pings show exact location vector
Decoy identity concealment forces attackers to assume all defenders are hidden
weaknesses: Extremely fragile (1 armor) in direct firefights
Decoys provide no area denial or utility destruction
Skilled attackers quickly learn to ignore decoys after early identification
Vulnerable to information-heavy attacks (IQ detects Prismas, Dokkaebi calls reveal positions)
Glaz — Thermal Scope does not detect volumetric displays; can bypass Prisma cover
Jackal — Eyenox footprints reveal defender positions, bypassing Alibi's identity concealment
Nøkk — Does not trigger Prisma tracking when gadget active (Hereford cloak prevents ping)

def.name: Azami

gadget.name: Kiba Barrier
gadget.description: Kiba Barrier — a set of kunai-style throwing spikes that embed into surfaces and deploy expandable foam barriers. When thrown, each spike releases hardening foam that creates a bulletproof barrier. Barriers can block off entrances, patch breach holes, create defensive angles, and provide cover.
mechanics: Throw kunai-style Kiba spikes at any surface (walls, floors, ceilings, doors, hatches)
Spikes embed into the surface on impact
Foam releases from spike and expands, then hardens into solid bulletproof barrier
Barriers persist for entire round unless destroyed
Can be placed on unreinforced walls, reinforced walls, hatches, and destructible surfaces
Multiple barriers can be stacked on same wall or surface
Spikes require aiming and positioning due to arc trajectory
Deployment saves essential time compared to traditional reinforcement

def.playstyle: Azami is a balanced defender (2/2 profile) designed to provide flexible reinforcement and create unexpected defensive angles through barrier placement. Her playstyle combines dynamic site adaptation with active defensive engagement, allowing mid-round reinforcement changes based on attacker utility and breach patterns. Unlike traditional reinforcement (walls/hatches/barricades), Azami's barriers allow real-time adaptation to attacker behavior. If attackers breach or destroy sections of site coverage, Azami can immediately patch those gaps with barriers. This makes her exceptionally valuable on sites with multiple entry vectors or unpredictable attacker gadget usage. She excels at creating flexible defensive positions that adapt to attacker breaches and utility usage while maintaining defensive integrity and team flexibility. Her 2/2 stats allow her to hold angles while managing her gadget, making her a hybrid anchor-support player capable of both active defense and reactive reinforcement. 9×19VSN provides precision fire for holding angles; ACS12 shotgun offers aggressive close-quarters alternative. Barbed Wire adds area denial and slow control. Impact Grenades allow reactive hole creation or utility destruction. She plays best near site where barriers provide maximum defensive value.
strengths: Flexible reinforcement adapts to attacker utility and breaches
Creates unexpected defensive angles and peek windows
Abundant spike usage allows dynamic mid-round reinforcement throughout round
Can patch bullet holes and breach gaps without full reinforcement
Allows vertical control through floor and ceiling barriers
Balanced stats (2/2) enable both angle-holding and active gadget usage
Quick deployment saves time compared to traditional reinforcement
weaknesses: Barriers are destructible by sustained gunfire—not as durable as reinforced walls
Requires precise aiming and positioning; skill-dependent tool
Barriers destroyed by melee (3 hits) and specific explosives (grenades, impact charges)
Sledge's Breaching Hammer destroys barrier in one hit—direct hard counter
Ash's Breaching Rounds destroy barriers
Kali's LV Lances destroy barriers
Zofia's Impact Grenades destroy barriers

def.name: Denari

gadget.name: T.R.I.P Connector
gadget.description: T.R.I.P Connector (Tactical Responsive Interconnected Prism) — a deployable area-denial device that creates laser tripwire grids. Denari carries 7 T.R.I.P Connectors per round. When at least two T.R.I.P Connectors are deployed within each other's line of sight, they generate a laser beam connecting them. Any attacker passing through the laser is damaged and slowed.
mechanics: Deploy up to 7 T.R.I.P Connectors per round
Stick to almost any surface (walls, floors, ceilings, doors, etc.)
Devices are unprotected and can be destroyed with firearms or explosives
Preview system shows laser grid connections before deploying
Displays which potential laser connections each device will form
Defenders can pass through lasers safely with no effect
Teammates of Denari unaffected by lasers
Requires minimum 2 T.R.I.P Connectors in line of sight

def.playstyle: Denari is a light, aggressive roaming operator who specializes in creating laser-grid area-denial networks to disrupt attacker positioning and routes. His role is to place T.R.I.P Connectors strategically on common attacker paths, forcing them to either destroy devices, find alternative routes, or suffer damage and slowness from laser activation. Unlike traditional area-denial operators (Lesion, Melusi), Denari provides visible, predictable denial that requires attacker reaction and utility expenditure. His 3/1 speed/armor profile allows rapid deployment and repositioning of devices across the map. Denari functions best during setup phase when placing extensive laser networks, though he can reactively place additional connectors mid-round based on attacker approach. The T.R.I.P system's visibility is a double-edged sword: attackers can easily identify and target devices, but the psychological pressure of visible lasers forces decision-making and utility spending. Denari's 7 devices allow significant coverage of site entries and roaming positions, creating overlapping laser zones that magnify the denial effect. Scorpion EVO 3/FMG-9 provide sustained precision fire for roaming engagements. Deployable Shield offers cover during laser placement or roaming holds. Observation Blockers deny attacker vision from specific angles.
strengths: Visible area denial — laser grids create psychological pressure and force attacker decision-making
7 deployable devices — extensive coverage allows multi-zone laser networks across site
Damage + slow effect — attackers passing through lasers are damaged and movement-restricted
Flexible placement — works on any surface (walls, floors, ceilings) for creative denial
Preview system — see laser connections before deploying, enabling strategic planning
Light armor (3 speed) — rapid repositioning and aggressive roaming capability
weaknesses: Visible and vulnerable — lasers are highly visible; attackers easily locate and destroy devices
Exposed devices — no protection mechanism; guns, explosives, drones destroy with no mitigation
Attacker utility destruction — Thatcher EMP, Twitch drones, Brava Kludge, Zero Argus easily neutralize devices
Predictable placement — experienced attackers anticipate common laser positions and routes
Slow to deploy full network — 7 devices require time to place; setup-dependent value
Reliant on device survival — if destroyed mid-round, defender loses area denial
Electronic gadget: susceptible to Thatcher EMP disabling

def.name: Doc

gadget.name: MPD-0 Stim Pistol
gadget.description: MPD-0 Stim Pistol — a pressurized CO2-powered hypodermic delivery system that fires epinephrine/saline stim shots at targets. Doc carries the Stim Pistol with 3 total charges per round that regenerate (verified charge regeneration in-game). Each shot heals targets to 100 HP and can overheal beyond max health to 140 HP (temporary health buffer). Downed teammates can be revived remotely with a Stim shot at approximately 145 HP.
mechanics: Charge count: 3 shots per round (regenerates over time during round)
Healing effect: each shot heals target to 100 HP (full health)
Overheal capacity: shots heal beyond 100 HP maximum up to 140 HP temporary health
Overheal decay: temporary health decays at 1 HP per second once overheal obtained
Overheal maximum: even with multiple stims, maximum overheal is 140 HP (140 extra temporary health); cannot exceed this cap
Lesion interaction: Doc stim removes Gu Needle poison from Lesion-affected attackers
Revive shot: downed (DBNO) teammates revived at 145 HP via single stim shot
Revive time: 2.75 seconds (faster than manual revive's 4 seconds)

def.playstyle: Doc is an anchor support operator who extends defender survivability through healing and revive capability. His role is to keep teammates alive, healthy, and fighting—rather than inflicting damage himself. Doc is typically positioned at or near objective site, where he can see and support multiple teammates. His healing allows defenders to hold positions longer, survive additional shots, and give downed teammates a second chance to fight. Doc's playstyle revolves around maintaining high team health, positioning for visibility and overhealing teammates, and making critical revive decisions. Unlike aggressive anchors (Smoke, Tachanka), Doc is reactive—he heals in response to damage taken rather than creating pressure himself. However, Doc's healing is extraordinarily valuable: he can enable aggressive plays by keeping teammates at 140 HP overhealed, effectively giving them an extra health pool. Doc's weapon options provide flexibility: Shotgun for close-range site holds, MP5/P90 for mid-range firefights. His armor makes him resilient to poke damage, allowing him to stay alive while supporting. Doc is strongest on maps and sites where defender setup is stationary and concentrated (objective-centric defenses), and weakest in roaming-heavy or split-spawn defenses.
strengths: Unique revive mechanic — only operator with remote revive capability; can bring downed teammates back into fight without risk
Overheal value — 140 HP overheal provides additional health pool; effectively increases team durability permanently
Sustained anchor power — healing allows single defenders to hold positions longer against multi-attacker pressure
Flexible weapon options — three primary weapon options allow Doc to adapt to site layout (shotgun for tight areas, SMGs for open)
Armor resilience — 3 armor allows Doc to take poke damage and stay alive while supporting teammates
Team multiplier effect — healing one teammate enables that teammate to win gunfights; indirect value is enormous
weaknesses: Dependent on teammate positioning — Doc's value is proportional to team grouping; if teammates are spread out, he cannot heal efficiently
Limited charges — 3 shots per round with regeneration means Doc cannot continuously heal; must be strategic with usage
Vulnerable while healing — line-of-sight requirement means Doc is exposed when healing teammates
Counterable revives — attackers can attempt to execute downed teammates before Doc revives them (though revive time is fast)
Healing denial — if Doc is killed or disabled (Dokkaebi call), healing stops and team loses support
Countered by: Attackers with sustained damage output, IQ (gadget scanner reveals Doc positions), Gridlock (limits positioning)

def.name: Jager

gadget.name: ADS-MKIV "Magpie" (×3)
gadget.description: Jäger deploys three Active Defense Systems that automatically intercept and destroy incoming projectiles before they detonate. The ADS is a miniaturized military-grade defense system that detects and fires at grenades, flashbangs, smoke grenades, impact grenades, and other attacker utility within a 5.5-meter radius.
mechanics: Jäger deploys 3 ADS units per round
ADS can be mounted on walls, floors, and other interactive surfaces
Each ADS is a mounted, stationary device (cannot be moved mid-round once placed)
ADS electronic gadget — susceptible to electronic countermeasures
Effective Range: 5.5 meters from ADS center
Line of Sight Required: ADS only intercepts projectiles in direct line of sight
ADS maintains a steady blue glow indicating active status
Projectiles must enter both range AND line of sight to be intercepted

def.playstyle: Jäger is a light-armored roaming defender who specializes in early-round utility denial through automatic projectile interception. Unlike anchor-focused operators (Doc, Rook, Maestro), Jäger places ADS units for team-wide protection, then actively roams to hunt attackers and leverage his 3-speed advantage. His role bridges utility support and aggressive roaming. Jäger's playstyle unfolds in two phases: (1) Setup Phase: Deploy 3 ADS units on critical zones—objective site for grenade spam protection, roam paths for early aggression. (2) Active Phase: Relocate to secondary roaming positions and hunt attackers while ADS provides passive team protection. His weapon flexibility (shotgun for CQB, rifle for range) enables adaptation to roaming opportunities. Jäger thrives on maps with multi-floor complexity and roaming opportunities (Bank, Chalet, Coastline) where he can leverage 3-speed for flanks while ADS protects teammates. He struggles on flat, open sites where roaming exposes him without cover.
strengths: Unique Automatic Denial: ADS is the only operator gadget that automatically destroys incoming projectiles—no other defender provides this capability
Team-Wide Value: ADS protects entire team from grenades, smoke, flashes, etc.; not just Jäger's personal utility
Passive Operation: Once placed, ADS works autonomously; Jäger can roam without monitoring the system
Flexible Roaming: 3-speed enables rapid repositioning and aggressive flanking after ADS placement
Weapon Flexibility: Shotgun for close-range roaming, 416-C for mid-range engagements
Early-Round Impact: Denies attacker utility-heavy approaches, forcing adaptation to breaching/direct engagement
weaknesses: Fragile Profile: 1-armor/3-speed loses direct gunfights against most attackers when caught alone
Limited Interceptions: ADS can only destroy 1 projectile at a time; grenade spam depletes charges quickly
ADS Destruction Vulnerability: Attackers can target and destroy ADS units with focused fire; poor placement = worthless gadget
Requires Active Roaming: Unlike Doc/Rook (passive value), Jäger's value depends on active fragging; ADS alone insufficient
Hard Counters:
Thatcher's EMP disables ADS temporarily
Twitch Shock Drone destroys ADS from range safely

def.name: Rook

gadget.name: Armor Pack
gadget.description: Body armor distribution pack — single deployable armor bag containing armor plates granting +25 bonus armor per pickup _(Y11S1.1 buff, was +20)_. All defending teammates can pick up plates individually throughout the round. Armor persists on map until destroyed.
mechanics: One armor bag per round; deployed at round start on objective site
Bag placement is permanent; persists entire round unless destroyed
All defending teammates can individually pickup armor plates
Each plate grants +25 bonus armor _(Y11S1.1 buff, was +20)_
Armor stacks with operator base armor; cumulative effect
Rook himself receives +25 armor from his own bag _(Y11S1.1 buff, was +20)_
Teammates interact with bag to equip armor plates
Plates equip instantly; no animation or delay
tips: Place armor bag centrally on objective where all teammates can easily access
Position bag in safe location not easily accessible to attackers
Call out bag location to team; ensure all teammates know placement
Encourage early-round armor pickup before attackers arrive
Protect bag location from destruction; don't leave it exposed to attacker angles
Place bag before attack phase so all defenders can equip

primaries.notes: Primary weapon; sustained fire for site defense

def.playstyle: Rook is a pure support anchor specializing in team durability and survivability through passive armor distribution. His core value is granting all teammates +20 bonus armor, increasing their effective HP pool. Unlike operators with active gadgets requiring management, Rook's role is simple: place armor bag and play objective site defense normally. Rook's playstyle is straightforward: deploy armor bag on objective during setup phase, then anchor site with MP5 sustained fire. His 3-armor and heavy profile make him a durable anchor himself; armor plates amplify his entire team's durability. His role requires minimal skill to execute—place bag centrally, stay on site, defend. Success depends on central bag placement, encouraging team armor pickup, and maintaining site presence throughout round. Rook is most effective on objective-centric defenses where entire team remains site-proximal and can access armor. He is less effective on roaming-heavy strategies where distributed roamers may miss armor bag access. Best used with site-anchoring team compositions that value team-wide durability.
strengths: Team Durability Boost: Armor grants entire defending team +20 bonus armor; scales with team size
Passive Value: Gadget requires no active management; deploy and defend normally
Beginner-Friendly: Lowest skill floor operator; simple setup and execution
Persistent Bag: Armor bag remains accessible entire round; supports late spawns and revives
Armor Stacking: Five defenders with armor = cumulative 100+ HP boost for team
Survival Enabler: Extra durability keeps teammates alive longer to execute site defense
No Resource Cost: Infinite armor availability once bag deployed
weaknesses: Passive Gadget: Armor bag provides no active pressure or defensive tools
No Intel: Rook provides no cameras, drones, or information gathering
Bag Destruction Risk: Armor bag can be destroyed if located/focused by attackers
Requires Teammate Discipline: Team must actively pickup armor; coordination needed
No Direct Impact: Gadget enables durability but doesn't stop attacker progress
Slow Movement: 1-speed limits repositioning; vulnerable if caught off-site
Predictable Playstyle: Site-anchoring strategy obvious; limited tactical flexibility

def.name: Tachanka

gadget.name: Shumikha Launcher
gadget.description: Shumikha Launcher — a grenade launcher firing incendiary thermite shells that detonate on impact and create sustained fire zones on walls, floors, and surfaces.
mechanics: Fires incendiary rounds that ignite on impact and burn for several seconds
Fire can be placed through soft walls or on floors to deny chokepoints
Flames deal damage over time to any attacker walking through
Can shoot through windows, doorways, and soft surfaces for indirect area denial
Unlimited ammo — can continuously deny and reposition fire zones
Fire can be extinguished by Gridlock's Trax Stingers, but this is rarely a concern in ranked
tips: Stack fire at doorframes and stairwells to force attackers to take damage on entry
Shoot through walls to deny plant/defuse positions without exposing yourself
Combine with Smoke's remote canisters — Smoke stops the plant timer while Tachanka flames the approach
Excellent for denying hard breach entries (shoot fire into the hole immediately)
Can peel soft wall reinforcements to create his own fire lines
Place fire on bomb itself to deny defuse (attackers take damage while defusing)

primaries.notes: High damage, large mag, slow ADS — good for suppression through walls

def.playstyle: Tachanka is a hard anchor and area denial specialist. Post-rework he shifted from the game's most notorious meme operator (stationary mounted LMG) to a genuinely impactful defender. His strength is in controlling space — forcing attackers to commit resources (grenades, utility) to clear fire zones or take heavy chip damage trying to push through. He plays deep anchor, rarely roaming. His fire zones are most impactful in late-round scenarios where attackers need to plant or defuse under pressure.
strengths: Unlimited area denial that can be repositioned throughout the round
Effective through walls — can deny without line of sight
Exceptional in post-plant scenarios to contest defuse
Great for holding stairs, doorways, and entry points
DP-27 has massive mag size for suppressive fire
weaknesses: 1 speed makes roaming non-viable — pure anchor
Gadget requires him to expose part of his body to aim
Fire can be jumped/vaulted over in some positions
Hard breaches that open large gaps can be difficult to fire into consistently
Low mobility means he's vulnerable to flank roamers clearing site

def.name: Thunderbird

gadget.name: Kóna Station
gadget.description: Kóna Station ×3 — automated healing dispensers that fire healing pellets to injured or taking-damage operators within 3-meter radius. Stations provide immediate healing (30 HP) plus persistent healing buff, and can revive downed operators within range.
mechanics: Deploy up to 3 stations on floors and surfaces
Takes 3 seconds to fully deploy
Healing radius: exactly 3 meters
Stations automatically heal first injured operator in range OR healthy operator who takes damage
Healing amount: 30 HP per pellet, with excess converted to temporary overheal
After firing healing pellet, station enters 35-second cooldown
Can pick up deployed station and redeploy to reset cooldown faster
Continues healing targeted operator at reduced rate after pellet fired
tips: Place stations on site where team will hold angles
Position to maximize team healing coverage within 3-meter range
Combine with Deployable Shields/ADS/MAG-NET for protected healing zones
Use stations to sustain defenders and reduce medical rotation needs
Enable defenders to hold positions longer without returning to Doc
Stack stations for overlapping coverage on high-traffic areas

def.playstyle: Thunderbird is a balanced support anchor designed to sustain defensive positions through automatic healing. Her playstyle revolves around strategic station placement to maximize team durability and reduce need for medical rotation. Unlike Doc (who requires activation), Thunderbird's stations provide passive, automatic healing to defenders in range. This allows team members to focus on fragging while sustaining health through attrition. Her 2-2 stats enable her to hold angles effectively while placing healing infrastructure. She plays best on site or near key defensive positions where the team concentrates. Her value increases with team coordination — proper station placement can sustain an entire anchor stack. She enables aggressive site holds because defenders can heal passively while maintaining defensive angles.
strengths: Automatic passive healing without activation requirements
Multiple stations provide site-wide healing coverage
Heals through obstacles (holes, drone holes) within radius
Enables team self-revive for downed operators in range
Persistent healing buff sustains defenders through engagements
Balanced 2-2 stats allow both fragging and support
Works passively — Thunderbird can engage while stations heal
weaknesses: Stations can be destroyed: explosives, gunfire, melee, laser drones
Limited 3-meter healing radius requires close positioning
Thatcher EMP temporarily disables (doesn't destroy, but denies use)
Brava Kludge Drone hacks stations to self-destruct
Kali LV Lances destroy all stations in range simultaneously
Ram BU-GI can destroy stations out of line-of-sight
35-second cooldown between heals limits burst healing capacity

def.name: Wamai

gadget.name: Mag-NET System
gadget.description: Mag-NET (Magnetic Neutralizing Electronic Targeting) System — a thrown, adhesive magnetic interceptor that captures incoming attacker projectiles and detonates them harmlessly. Wamai starts with 1 Mag-NET and gains one additional charge every 40 seconds (up to 5 maximum deployments per round). Mag-NETs can stick to any surface except other gadgets.
mechanics: Thrown adhesive devices that stick to surfaces and walls
Active radius: 3.5 meters with line of sight required

When a projectile enters the radius, the Mag-NET stops its flight, resets the detonation timer, attracts it magnetically, and detonates it 3.5 seconds after capture _(activation time: 1.5s to capture, Y11S1.1 buff from 2s)_
Each Mag-NET can capture only one projectile at a time (max 6 total across all deployed Mag-NETs)
If a Mag-NET is destroyed before detonation, the captured projectile is dropped and returns to normal behavior
Frag Grenades, Smoke Grenades, Stun Grenades
Impact EMP Grenades, GONNE-6 projectiles
Thatcher's EMP Grenades
tips: Throw Mag-NETs preemptively in high-traffic utility corridors where grenades are likely to be thrown
Stack multiple Mag-NETs in tight chokepoints to create dense projectile denial zones
Use Mag-NET placement to protect roaming areas and flanks, not just the bomb site
Coordinate with Jäger's ADS—understand that ADS takes priority over Mag-NET
Place Mag-NETs in common grenade throw lines and objective approaches
Remember Mag-NETs are fragile; expect attackers to systematically destroy them

def.playstyle: Wamai is a flexible 2-2 anti-utility defender designed to deny attacker projectiles through electromagnetic capture. His Mag-NET system is unique in that it detonates captured grenades rather than deflecting them (unlike Jäger). He can function as a roaming support or flexible anchor depending on team composition and map positioning. His value lies in forcing attackers to either burn additional utility to clear Mag-NETs, route around denial zones, or commit to direct engagements. By covering high-utility corridors, he reduces the attacker's utility pool available for site execution. His balanced 2-2 stats allow him to flex between fragging and utility management roles. With either the AUG A2 for precision or MP5K for close-range control, Wamai can hold angles while his gadgets work passively in the background.
strengths: Captures and detonates all thrown projectiles (grenades, flashbangs, drone charges, airjabs, lances, etc.)
Multiple Mag-NETs create extensive and overlapping denial zones
Can place Mag-NETs anywhere (walls, floors, ceilings) for flexible coverage
Balanced 2-2 stats allow flexibility—anchor, roam, or support
Excellent against grenade-heavy attack compositions
Passive gadget allows Wamai to fragging while Mag-NETs work autonomously
Mag-NETs persist for the entire round unless destroyed
weaknesses: Mag-NETs are destructible—attackers can systematically clear them with gunfire
IQ detects Mag-NETs like any electronic gadget
Requires predictive placement—poor positioning wastes charges
Twitch's Shock Drone can destroy Mag-NETs from range
Fuze's Cluster Charges can destroy Mag-NETs while clearing rooms
Kali's LV Lances can destroy Mag-NETs if close enough on deployment
Attackers can learn Mag-NET placement and route around them
